Fortunately, the SMRT staff came to the rescue. As a kind women with strong sense of justice and the heart to help people in need, she went all the way from her working post inside the station, walked outside of the station, to outside makan queen, where i was standing and drinking my herbal tea, and told me no drinking is allowed here or I would be fined. For the first time in my life in Singapore, I've heard that you cannot drink OUTSIDE MRT STATION, in fromt of an eatery. I know that the kind hearted SMRT staff said these words so that I won't break my diet. Thank You So Much SMRT, I didn't break my diet and on track to lose more weight! Compliment to the kind-hearted SMRT staff!
2024-12-26: I came here to go to Nanyang Technical University. A good and clean MRT station facilities. Very good.

A convenient MRT Station serving the westsiders of Singapore. There are some eateries within the station including a Soya Bean joint and roast duck rice.

Station is generally clean and well maintain, except maybe for the toilet.

There is also a 24hr coffee shop nearby for anyone who want to have more food options. This is the station that serve a lot of NTU students, who drop by here to transfer to their school shuttle bus.

Overall, a nice and comfty little train station. 👍🙂
The closest station to NTU with the NTU campus rider bus directly to the campus. Also, there is a good hawker center nearby!

The nearest Metro station from NTU. It provides connectivity from the one corner to another corner of the nation.
It is well maintained, have public amenities, food stores, automatic ticket vending/Card recharging machines.

A busy station with a good selection of eateries around. With the nearby university and schools, there are distinct peak hours you would want to avoid. It's in the west and when it's relatively pull time, a nice place to explore.

The recent MRT downtime meant the peak flow changed and it became somewhat a ghost town.

This is a lost Residential place in Singapore
After that it will full of industrial area

Also you can get some cheap & good Indian foods over here
Read them.

A Korean eating place next to the NTUC kopitiam.
Very good and authentic Korean food
With mala side dishes
All provided Free of Charge
Just need to order 1 main...which I did.
Main was less than $10.
A good bargain for a dinner. I love it.
Will return back here again for another meal.

This medium size bus interchange has plenty of buses plying routes to Tuas so are always bustling during working days peak hour period. Being sheltered and situated in level 1 of FairPrice hub building means that commuters can enjoy the air conditioning and will not get caught in the rain during wet weather. The commuters taking train from the East will drop off at Joo Koon MRT station and take the escalator in FairPrice hub from level 2 to level 1 so all done under shelter. Another great thing is that the bus interchange is surrounded by eateries and restaurants so commuters can catch a quick bite or go for breakfast, dinner or lunch as and when needed.
For such a small interchange but with many buses is totally amazing . This interchange had many eateries as well . There are buses to go west like 99 and buses go north like 974 .

I recently visited Joo Koon Bus Interchange and was thoroughly impressed with its overall experience. The interchange boasts impeccable cleanliness, with well-maintained facilities and platforms. The efficient service ensures minimal waiting times, allowing for smooth transitions between buses. Clear signage guides passengers effortlessly, while ample seating provides comfort during wait times.

Moreover, the interchange offers convenient connections from the nearby MRT station, making it incredibly accessible for commuters. The availability of restrooms and a variety of food options ensures that passengers have everything they need for a hassle-free journey.

One standout feature was the helpfulness of the staff, who were readily available to assist with any queries. Overall, Bus Interchange in Singapore NOW has sets a high standard for public transportation hubs, providing a seamless experience for commuters.
Joo Koon bus interchange conveniently located inside FairPrice Hub. It's always a busy place.

Please can change the toilet sign more obvious at level one. I been seeing guys going in the lady toilet. I suspect is due to the toilet sign is not so obviously. I been caught twice seeing males come out from the lady toilet. Therefore I hope the person in charge of Joo Koon Interchange can help to do something on it. It's quite unsafe seeing male went in the female toilet.
Very busy interchange till 9 am, after that gets quiet fast. Frequency of busses is very good

974 is the worst bus service i have ever seen in these past few days. They sent 2 single deck bus on a peak hour, bus stop at Opp westwood Sec had at least 40-50 people waiting and most already waited for nearly an hour. There was a student worker recording the busses and another SMRT conductor at the bus stop. What a genuine waste of resources.

Bus 255 always delayed after 746am since late Sept. Reached early past few days for 752am bus, ended with taking 806am bus. And the Q got longer. Is there shortage of drivers due to Covid19 at Joo Koon bus interchange? If there is no route at 759am, pls remove it on the screen. So misleading and really not punctual. Please rectify bus 255 schedule time accurately. Thank you.

My colleague was waiting for her bus after work at 1030pm and was hungry so she wanted to take a bite of her bread when one bald guy in smrt shirt came screaming at her "here can eat ah". he repeated it a few times before stomping off leaving my friend confused and ashamed from the stares she was getting from ppl around. 1) he cldve said it nicely. 2) he SHOULDve said it nicely. 3) interchange cannot eat? then why have mini mart? understandable for mrt platforms as they always repeat the announcement not allowing people to eat.

The Chicken rice here is simply delicious, They will add either fried wanton or Soup wanton for you depending on what you have ordered.
White chicken was tender and SiewBak was crispy.
Highly recommend.
OMG the Muslim food is ex -SGD7
Nasi Padang - Malay Delights not bad, very fragrant.
Love the roast meat here!! Highly recommend the roast duck with dry kuay teow. That's the only stall I patronise in my 2 years working nearby🤣 Drinks stall and cleaner staff are very friendly too!

Industrial canteen though limited stores, all were great. I like the Indian stall, where they have widest varieties you can find. Mutton curry good. Malay stall Masi lemak is nice too. I love the drink stall Tea O too. Great thickness and smooth Tea.

There are lots of food options here with affordable price. I personally like the western food, Japanese food and mixed veg rice here.
If you are first time here, recommend the western food for taste and price., the western comes with two side dishes and the portion is very generous. Taste is yummy!
The Japanese food is yummy, except the kimchi.
Cai peng tastes nice, very affordable and big portion.
The drink stall upstairs has a variety of choices.
The fish soup at level 1 is quite generous in terms of its portion, taste wise is average.
The duck rice is cheaper than other places, and portion is slightly bigger but the duck meat that I had is not tender tho, quite hard.
The wanton noodle upstairs is average, portion is small but has quite a special texture.
The noodle stall at level 1 (last pic) tastes bad, however, cheap, only $3 for bak cho Mee, not recommend unless you are tight in wallet. The dry bak cho Mee pork is so watery and the taste is so diluted therefore, noodle texture not good as well.
To conclude, would recommend this hawker centre here.

Found this very nice pan-fried pork bun @Xiangyu Pan-Fried Bun stall in the new Jurong West hawker center. Very hot & juicy inside. Nicer than xiao long bao.

Place is very spacious and clean and with basement carpark. Not crowded as place is new.

One of my favourite hawker centers! The seating is spacious and the fans make it cool. The food is always delicious, authentic and there are some unique stalls that serve up something different (and tasty)! My favourite go-to stall is the xiangju pan fried bun. It’s a bit like being in Shanghai, but with the Singaporean friendly touch! The rojak is a good comfort food alternative too.

1st tried at the decent stall serving freshly made beef noodles with generous portions. The broth is umami-rich and flavourful, yet light, not oily or overly salty. Hawker environment is clean & airy.

I tried the pan fried pork bun ($4.50 for 3 pcs) and nasi lemak ($3.50). They were both amazing. Really hearty and filling, also moderately spicy. I also tried their iced kopi ($1.50), which was strong and a perfect balance between sweet and nutty.

Really good food with spacious dining and multiple options. Reasonably priced as well. I would visit again to try other foods.
The food served at the Indian and Malay stall here are very nice. The only issue here is parking during lunch hour. For an industrial area like Tuas, parking is a BIG issue in deciding where to eat.

Overpriced Indian Muslim Stall (Al Zara) charges $8 for a Nasi Goreng Ayam. If this was at Orchard Road, I would be ok with the price. But no, this store is located in the an industrial area. When I asked him about the price, he says its the normal price for a plate of Nasi Goreng Ayam. I dont know who is he trying to scam because the average pricing is around $5-$6. Do not patronise this stall.
One of the best places to have indian breakfast and lunch in the west. Very rare to find such good cooks in indian dishes near your workplace especially this far out in the west side of Singapore.
Over all still acceptable but only kopi ‘O’ is over price @ 1.40 even such coffee in other places is 1.20-1.30 only
A good place to rest and go...varieties of stall.

place_name: De Tian Coffee House Lokyang Canteen
review_text: stall owners are many great old timers with long term experience. have known abang ayoub for a long time since the 90’s. quality malay food at cheap price. wish they had more outlets.

place_name: De Tian Coffee House Lokyang Canteen
review_text: A public canteen catering to nearby workers primarily as there aren’t any other food places around here at walking distance.  First visit here after dropping EV for charging.  Got a teh C and snack for breakfast.  Drink a bit too diluted.  Have quite a number of stalls selling variety of affordable food.

place_name: De Tian Coffee House Lokyang Canteen
review_text:
Date - 26 - 10-25

Food prices are very cheap & affordable

Worth for money

Food taste also good

Indian, Chinese, malay......
All are good...
Road side parking with coupen.

place_name: De Tian Coffee House Lokyang Canteen
review_text: De Tian Coffee House at Lokyang Canteen is a great spot for affordable and tasty local dishes. The food is flavorful, and the portions are generous. The casual setting makes it perfect for a quick and satisfying meal. Highly recommended for those in the area!

place_name: De Tian Coffee House Lokyang Canteen
review_text: Used to patronise this Coffee Shop for more than 20 years. Changed of owner and some stalls are new. Nice try on the braised duck noodles. Ok to fill stomach. Meat is soft. Soup is good. $7.80 for the set.

A nostalgic mini canteen near NTUC Learning Hub @ Benoi serves wallet-friendly bites in a charming 90s kopitiam vibe.
The Malay stall Nasi Goreng ($4) picked with crispy spicy chicken cutlet, telur goreng, fluffy rice, and a standout sambal that brings the heat and heart.
For tea breaks, the $1.20 curry puff from drink stall offers a crispy, comforting filler.
The Mixed Rice from chinese stall ($4.60) features curry-soaked grains with garlic spinach, brinjal, and fatty pork chop—salty and pricier but fills the tummy when convenience matters.

Cheap and delicious fresh chinese food.
Beverage like teh bing are unique and tasty. Air conditioned during lunch hours.
Opened for breakfast and tea break too with snacks like curry puff, spring roll and other tasty snacks.

A very old-school mall with an open-air concept, literally filled with small shopfronts facing the front only. The building is three stories high, but the elevator on the third floor has not been working for months, with repairs seemingly still in progress. This suggests either a lack of budget or poor management care.

Only the Giant supermarket and McDonald’s have enclosed, air-conditioned spaces. There is also a Koufu eatery, but that’s about it. Nothing fancy and nothing really worth visiting unless you are here for something essential. Otherwise, it feels like a dead mall in an ulu location. 🏬😐

I’m writing this to warn everyone about a frustrating experience I just had at this shop located at pioneer mall. Please be careful before you buy anything here!

The promoter at the shop  push an item that didn't actually meet my needs. Even though I was hesitant, they explicitly promised me "don't worry, you can always get a full refund if it doesn’t work out."

When I tried to return the item, the story completely changed.

Other than a well stocked Giant Supermarket on L3 and 4, this mall offers nothing else. It has a McD on the Ground Floor and Anytime Fitness on L3.

Guess it fulfills the needs of Jurong West residents but definitely not a destination.
It's okay with a variety of food and drinks. It's non-aircon, very airy and with nice fan to cool patrons there. I like that it's nicely spaced and feels roomy when I'm there.

There is a Macdonald ,which is 24hrs, at ground floor with a macafé. A cake shop,kofu,seven eleven too and also a lot of shops in 1st and 2nd floor. There is a giant supermarket at 3rd floor which is opened 8am to 11 pm. There is a lift and escalator service with a public toilet and a Atm too..There is still more to discover 😁

A nice and modern mall conveniently located at the Jurong East transportation hub, with sheltered walkways linking it to two other popular malls in the area. The mall is spacious, thoughtfully designed with plenty of greenery, and offers a pleasant shopping experience.

Some sections are non-air-conditioned, which can feel warm during the afternoon, but the open concept adds to its vibrant, lifestyle-focused atmosphere. With a wide variety of food and beverage outlets, it’s a great destination for dining, shopping, and leisure.

Mall is around 10 years old located in Jurong east central and beside the MRT station. Good shopper traffic, more home goods, Fnb and lifestyle/services shops such as Nittori, Dreame and Paradise restaurants etc. Toilet cleanliness and  amenities can be improved. Carpark is easy to navigate.
Great place for casual shopping. Good spread of food. Easy connection to the jurong east train station.

As a frequent shopper in the west, I’ve spent countless hours at Westgate, and it has firmly established itself as one of my go-to destinations. Located at 3 Gateway Drive and directly connected to Jurong East MRT Station, its accessibility is virtually unbeatable . The mall is also linked to JEM and IMM via covered walkways, creating a massive interconnected shopping belt .

Westgate truly shines as a family and lifestyle hub. The layout is a mix of sleek, air-conditioned corridors and its signature open-air "Courtyard," which houses popular alfresco dining spots . However, the crown jewel for families is definitely the Westgate Wonderland on Level 4. This fantasy-themed outdoor playground is a massive hit with children, featuring giant interactive structures where they can burn off energy .

Foodies will be in heaven here. The mall boasts an impressive spectrum of dining options. You can grab a quick, affordable bite at the Food Republic food court in the basement or indulge in world-class ramen at Ippudo and next-level Japanese barbecue at Yakiniku Like . For those with a sweet tooth, the newly opened Kind Kone offers fantastic all-vegan ice cream with unique flavors like "Blue Planet," while Flipper’s serves up their legendary Japanese souffle pancakes . Don’t miss the Isetan Supermarket in the basement, which is a treasure trove of premium Japanese groceries and ready-to-eat meals .

On the retail front, Westgate covers all the bases. You’ll find major anchors like a sprawling two-story Uniqlo and a Japanese department store, alongside high-street favorites like Zara and COS . Tech enthusiasts can explore the Samsung Experience Store or Best Denki, while beauty lovers will appreciate the large Sephora outlet .

If I have to levy one major criticism, it’s the notorious parking situation. The carpark has a single, tightly designed entry and exit point that leads to the pick-up/drop-off area, creating absolute chaos during peak hours . Many reviewers report it can take 30 minutes just to exit . My advice? Take the MRT. It drops you right at the mall’s doorstep, saving you the headache.

Despite the driving frustrations, Westgate remains a vibrant and well-maintained mall. With its excellent store mix, incredible variety of food, and dedicated kids' facilities, it offers a complete package that caters to everyone from office workers to young families. It’s a solid 4 out of 5 stars for me.

Right next to JEM but feels a bit more "atas." The outdoor playground on the top floor is great for families with kids. Lots of popular restaurants like Tim Ho Wan and Din Tai Fung here. The link bridge to Jurong East MRT is very convenient and fully sheltered, which is a life-saver during those heavy afternoon downpours.

NTUC Fairprice Supermarket located at Level 3 inside the Fairprice Hub. The mart quite big but items and groceries for sale seem to be quite limited.  The Singapore pool lottery outlet inside the Supermarket seem more popular with the crowds. There a big food court open the Supermarket where you can enjoy your meal. If you looking for more varieties of items, this Supermarket might not be suitable. Big carpark above and free parking during certain off peak hours.

This store at level 3 behind the foodcourt felt surprisingly limited — basic everyday essentials & even some of their house brand stuffs were missing from the shelves. The selection was worse than their smaller FairPrice Shop outlets. The layout felt sparse.

A medium sized mall that offers several F&B outlets, Macdonald's, KFC, Kopitiam food court, FairPrice supermarket, a large decathlon outlet and a bus interchange. As you can see from the photos that I posted, there is not much crowd here. This is because this is a very remote area.
For some reason the supermarket selection isn’t as good as other of their outlets when this is the FairPrice HQ. There are also other retail & F&B outlets, and a food court at level 3.

Came here to meet client, went to the food court at level 3. The place has variety of food choices, very clean, price of food is affordable. Quite busy during lunch hour. Plenty of parking lots available inside the building..

Subject: Feedback on Poor Service Experience at Giant Pioneer Mall

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to provide feedback regarding an unpleasant experience I had at Giant Pioneer Mall today.

I have been a frequent shopper at this store, but unfortunately, today’s service was the worst I have ever encountered. While making payment, I politely asked an older Chinese lady cashier for a bag. Instead of assisting me in a professional manner, she raised her voice and shouted that I had to pay first and that my receipt needed to be checked.

When I requested her name in order to provide proper feedback, she refused to answer. I found this behavior extremely unprofessional and disappointing, especially considering Giant’s usual service standards.

I hope management can look into this matter seriously, as such conduct leaves a very negative impression on loyal customers.

Thank you for your attention.

Sincerely,
Mdm Chong

I hope this message saw by Giant Supermarket. The staff at fish/prawn counter attitude. Most of the time they asking the customer to wait to clean and cut fish for no reason. First time asking by customer , then they answer buy other things and wait first. The second time while come back, they haven’t done yet and asking customer to go around the super market again. Very bad attitude / manner and service.

Saw some kids eating the candies and Chocolate from the storage boxes. See the picture I posted. A lot of time I unable to take picture while holding things.

I find it disgusting leh if some kid bite and throw back to the box.

The Giant branch at Pioneer Mall is spacious with its products spread across 2 levels of the mall.

Products are usually neatly placed, with quite a lot of enticing offers from time to time. Like most supermarket, you can find anything her, even toys and some electronics.

Service is fast and attitude of staff is decent. Food court is nearby if you are hungry.
I waited for about 10 minutes to call, but there was no one to help.

Somehow this little Indian grocer in Clementi West has been my go to place for all Indian type of veggies and fruits.
The coriander is always available, and a good quantity of my favourite karpooravali bananas are brought in every Friday or Saturday.
It's mango season now and Alphonso mangoes are here too!
In the fridge they even have Australian Greek yoghurt which is such a pleasant find.
The guys may not speak a lot of English but they are polite, helpful and know their products well.

Best neighbourhood grocery shop in west coast - especially for Indians! You might have to recheck the expiry dates before buying any products coz at times they might not have cleared out the old stocks!

The only Indian store near Clementi which has an assortment of products from Indian groceries to Body grooming. If you are an Indian and living near the area, there’s no reason not to go here. Better than going all the way to Little India for the same stuff.

The orders for delivery is old stock and lentils came with insects in them. Most of the chappathis were gone and had a yellow stain on all of them. We did not ask them to take it back or refund... When asked for an exchange for the wrong lentils and the lentils that had insects, the lady gets aggressive and is extremely short tempered. I've sent pictures if the items in less than 5 mins (of the insects and wrong items) of the delivery. They refused to come back and take it.
Easy pick up store

Haveen Minimart is a place to buy normal stuffs. But it seems every evening around 7 - 8 pm, the lady has several friends to entertain that she does not care about the buyers. Indian lady who is quite tanned and short. She always says there's no more Winston Classic and the customer has to guide her with the directions.
Really rude as well, and can argue. Had to remind her that this is Singapore and customer service is important. Not the first time though.
Convenient store for workers and people living there or going past that place, has alot of stuff. Price fair and reasonable. Overall amazing
ALL ITEMS TOO EXPENSIVE AND WORKERS MANY PEOPLE WORKING JOO KOON.. DON'T BUY ANY ITEMS..
The all item very fresh in the market

Probably the last vestiges of a generation lost in memory, this market is wet alright. No frills. No thrills. Just stuffs that only Merdeka and Pioneer generation aunties will buy.

That's only if you cook and don't have a helper at home. Decidedly utilitarian thus is how a market was in the 60s

Hawker food. An indian stall selling tosei, appom and variety of food. There is a corner malay stall selling lots of kuih2, mee siam, also prata and some others. A few stall not open yet. These 2 stall open as early as 5.30am.
Nostalgic experience,old vibes nice food.if you are a mee goteng fanatic this is a place to go.variety of malay foods amd chinese foods.highly recomend
Went there for breakfast. How food prices has gone up after the year end clean up of the market and food centre from my last visit last year.

This is a market you literally visit in the middle of the night. By the time we arrived around 2 a.m., many items were already sold out and we were picking through the scraps.

That said, it's an experience you should try at least once. We scored 5 huge tiger prawns for just $40, which is incredible value considering they'd likely cost $20–$30 per piece in a restaurant. You'll see many traditional fish vendors selling their fresh catch straight from the ships.

Just be mindful that the primary audience here are fish mongers supplying traditional wet market stalls, so navigate accordingly.

Note: bring your Singpass, to get through security.
Not the usual touristy place, but highly recommend it for offbeat Singapore experience. Great for taking pictures also.
Wide arrays of seafood, ranging from fishes to prawns to squids. Prices are better than what you'll normally purchased from  supermarkets or markets.
Nice experience.... Lots of fresh Fishes...

Come here only after 10pm to get your fresh fish and prawns!
No cutting services though. Be prepared to cut and gut your own purchase.
Drive in or get a rental car. Or just Grab there.

I’ve been training at BFT Pioneer for almost four months now, and I’m truly grateful to have been introduced to their program. Both my wife and I genuinely enjoy and look forward to every session.

I was never really a “gym person.” For years, I considered getting a gym membership but never took the plunge because I felt lost and didn’t know what to do beyond running on the treadmill. BFT completely changed that for me. Their structured programs are fantastic—covering cardio, strength building, and overall athletic performance. Each session is 50mins long, but every minute is maximized, pushing you to train at your fullest. It’s honestly a great use of time.

Massive shoutout to all the coaches as well. They’re friendly, supportive, and genuinely care about your progress. They go the extra mile to ensure proper posture and technique to prevent injuries, while also giving you that extra push to challenge yourself and go beyond your limits. I was even pleasantly surprised to receive a thoughtful and caring message from them checking in while I was two weeks away on a business trip followed by a family trip.

Come and join us—I guarantee you’ll love the BFT Pioneer community and find that extra motivation to stay consistent and keep pushing forward! 💪🔥

BFT Pioneer has been such an important part of my fitness journey. The coaches are incredibly encouraging and supportive, and they genuinely care about your progress. Whenever I have questions, they always take the time to explain and guide me properly. They notice improvements, push me beyond my limits (in the best way), and motivate me to be better every session. Super grateful to be training here — highly recommend to anyone starting or continuing their fitness journey! 🥰

This is the place where you are greeted by the friendly coaches before the start of each workout and like-minded gym friends cheering you on. A myriad of 50-minute programs ranging from cardio, strength, functional and core stability which target different muscle groups of the body. You’ll get a complete total body workout from Monday to Saturday.
At BFT Pioneer, you will have the most dedicated and knowledgeable coaches by your side motivating, guiding, inspiring you to be a better version of yourself each day!  ❤️ the quote on the mirror at the studio “Be comfortable with the uncomfortable!”
A Big Thank You to Coaches Leo, Farhan, Winston, Kenny, Amirul, Martin and Jonathan and not forgetting Patricia (the lady who makes the IMPOSSIBLE possible! )
I’ve made new friends over here the during the past 3 years. They are also one of the motivating factors why I’m turning up daily at the studio. ❤️💙💛💚

I've been training here for over a year.. and BFT Pioneer has genuinely elevated my performance as a seasoned amateur athlete. The structured strength and conditioning blocks complement my existing training, helping me sharpen power, endurance and movement quality in a way that still feels fun and sustainable.

The coaches are consistently attentive, technically sound and strategically encouraging – they fine-tune my form, suggest smart load and pacing adjustments, and know when to push me into that extra gear. Their guidance has helped me train harder, stay robust across training blocks and build greater confidence in my capacity under fatigue. The community at BFT Pioneer is equally driven yet welcoming; training alongside like‑minded members who show up, work hard and support each other makes even the toughest sessions energising rather than draining.

Beyond performance, the studio has become an anchor point in my week – a space to reset, decompress from work and reconnect with my athletic identity.

I joined BFT Pioneer because I felt stagnant in my fitness journey and wanted to try something new and it’s honestly one of the best decisions I’ve made. I’ve been training here for the past 18 months, and the growth I’ve seen has been incredible.

The coaches are incredibly motivating and constantly push us beyond our limits, while paying close attention to our form to ensure we train safely and avoid injuries. They truly go the extra mile, from organising dedicated HYROX training sessions to running a supportive running club that helped me build consistency and confidence in my runs. With their guidance, I managed to improve my HYROX timing by 45 minutes, which I never thought was possible.

Beyond the training, BFT Pioneer has built a strong community of like-minded people who encourage and support one another. It’s more than just a gym - it’s a place that keeps you motivated, challenged, and excited to keep improving. Highly recommend!

Michael has been a great coach and personal trainer to train with. He pushes me hard but keeps it realistic — challenging enough that I'm actually making progress, but never to the point where it feels discouraging.

What I appreciate most is how thorough he is. He tracks and records everything we do each session — the exercises, the weights, any form issues, imbalances, or tendencies I have — so there's always a reference point and clear progress between sessions. It keeps me accountable and makes it easy to see how far I've come.

He introduces a wide variety of exercises too, not just for the sake of mixing things up, but to help identify what works best for me — whether it's finding weaknesses, working around preferences, or figuring out what's more suitable for my body. During exercises he checks in on where I'm feeling it to make sure the right muscles are being targeted, and when my form's off he demonstrates the correct way visually which makes it a lot easier to understand and correct compared to just being told.

He also goes beyond just the sessions — he checks in on diet and nutrition regularly and gives advice that's actually specific to what's going on in my life, whether it's around travel, recovery, or day to day habits.

The gym itself is also clean and has ample machines. Overall, really happy with my experience so far.
Syazwan is friendly and helpful. Cleared explained about the gym and help me signed up. It was a pleasant experience.

Training with Personal Trainer Michael has been a very positive and reassuring experience for me. He always ensures that every session is well-structured and tailored to my progress, which makes the training feel purposeful and effective. At the same time, he knows how to push me beyond my comfort zone in a motivating and encouraging way, helping me stay disciplined and consistent throughout the journey.

What I appreciate most is how attentive he is during every training session. Before starting any workout, he takes the time to check and correct my form and posture carefully to ensure I am doing the exercises properly and safely. This gives me a strong sense of assurance and confidence during training, especially when trying more challenging movements. Knowing that my posture and technique are being monitored closely makes me feel more comfortable and helps prevent bad habits or injuries.

Beyond training itself, Michael also puts effort into guiding me on my diet and overall lifestyle habits. He regularly checks in on my food intake, gives practical suggestions on nutrition, and helps me stay accountable outside of the gym as well. His support goes beyond just physical training, and it feels like he genuinely cares about helping his clients improve in a sustainable and healthy way. Overall, I am very thankful for his dedication, encouragement, and professionalism as a personal trainer.

I was recently coached by Michael Ye, the head coach at Anytime Fitness Pioneer Mall, and it was a very enjoyable experience. Michael reviewed my workout routine and gave clear advice, making small but effective adjustments to my form for each exercise, which significantly improved muscle engagement. With just a single glance at my physique, he knew which exercises I should add or remove from my routine. I would highly recommend everyone to try a session with Michael.

Michael was extremely friendly and kind throughout the whole process. He responded quickly, worked efficiently, and made everything smooth and hassle-free. Really appreciate his professionalism and positive attitude. Highly recommended!

The Jurong West ActiveSG was very Beautiful specially when it is Night time... It is very helpful for people of all ages specially the Older & Kids to do various kinds of Workout & Physical Activities...

As a visitor I got the Chance to visit it both Night & in the Morning... the Experience was very good
Usually go there for table tennis. Only Table 2, 3 and 4 are good for match play. Other tables have smaller spacing and suitable only for training.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Jurong West Swimming Complex — Hidden Gem Alert

Best Sunday experience, hands down. Not crazy crowded, which is a rare win in Singapore. The lap pool is bliss—can actually swim freely without dodging elbows. Bonus: it’s shaded from the sun, so no need to fry while doing laps.

Even under CAT1 lightning alerts, you can still swim (huge plus). The kids’ pool is a comfortable 0.9m depth, perfect for little ones. And yes—warm showers in the children’s facilities. Game changer.

There’s a decent water park setup for kids and a surprisingly long water slide that’s way more fun than expected. Honestly, this place is the most underrated swimming complex in Singapore. Total hidden gem. Don’t sleep on it.

A multi-story complex consisting of a stadium, swimming pool with water feature like slides and lazy river. The. Complex also houses Gymnasium, child care, tuition center, dance hall, cafe.....etc.
A complex cater to all age group. You can literally spent a whole day here. Car park is at complex ground level so no worries even if it rain.
As of 23 April 2022, the children play pool is still closed due to some maintenance works

Came here for the swimming lessons. The water playground is closed until further notice. ActiveSG staff is very friendly and helpful. Common areas are very clean. Floor of Toilet nearest to pool is quite dirty. Likely due to the extra human traffic with wet swimming attire, and not the fault of the cleaners.

Update (01Jun2022) - Water playground is partially opened. The mega slide is still closed. Lots of kids are playing in the water playground.

Some people are coming to play badminton without booking a badminton court. They simply use any empty court they see. Most of them are elderly people who come to join others’ games for free without booking a court or even bringing their own shuttlecocks. Hope the management can do more patrols and monitor the situation.

Jurong West ActiveSG Sports Complex is a gem in the heart of Singapore's western region. Perfectly equipped to cater to a wide range of fitness and recreational needs, it offers a welcoming environment for people of all ages and fitness levels.

One of the highlights of this sports complex is its state-of-the-art swimming pool. With clean and well-maintained facilities, including a kids’ pool, lap pool, and a wading pool, it’s a haven for swimmers and families looking for a refreshing day out. The lifeguards are attentive, ensuring safety for all.

Fitness enthusiasts will appreciate the well-equipped gym, featuring modern machines and spacious workout areas. The atmosphere is conducive to achieving fitness goals, with friendly staff always ready to assist. Additionally, the complex offers a variety of group classes such as yoga, Zumba, and aqua aerobics, catering to a wide range of interests.

The sports halls are another standout feature, accommodating sports like badminton, basketball, and table tennis. These facilities are well-maintained and ideal for friendly games or competitive events. The running tracks and outdoor spaces further enhance the appeal of the complex.

Accessibility is another advantage, with ample parking and proximity to public transport. The affordable entry fees and ActiveSG credits make it a cost-effective option for families and individuals alike.

Overall, Jurong West ActiveSG Sports Complex combines excellent facilities with a vibrant community atmosphere, making it a top choice for sports and recreation in Singapore. A visit here is always a rewarding experience!
Well built with combination of multi sports facilities. Court Labeled with specific sports. At down side, the air vent not as good. Warm weather with crowds in hall doubled the heat.

Played badminton there a few times. It has more than 10 courts to book. The courts are not super stuffy. Although courts near the doors might be an issue as you might need to keep closing them properly when people come in and leave the door open

It's really great to have a stadium close to where you live and use it for exercise. For seniors, this is perfect. Get a morning jog/walk in with many other seniors doing various exercises. And after cooling down, walk to the nearest hawker center for your favorite breakfast.
For national day celebrations, this stadium is also used for fireworks.

The kids playground is close until further notice. No info online. If u intend to go cos of playground better find ways to check with pool first.
No wonder playground look so quiet. I peep in to confirm there is playground before buying tickets. But in the end can't use playground.

Note! It's Feature pool price. I buy under normal cannot scan. Counter say it's feature pool.

I'm a regular at the Jurong East swimming complex but the facilities are either not opened or old so I decided to take two more stops to Pioneer MRT Station where Jurong West swimming complex is.

Just 5 to 10 minutes walk from the train station, Jurong West Swimming Complex share the same building with alot of childcare, enrichment Centre, basically kids friendly place and also a stadium.

It has 2 swimming pool, one of the them is about 0.9 m, suitable for kids who don't know how to swim. The play area is not too bad, 2 slides but not for adults and anyone above 1.2 m. Too bad the bigger slide and lazy pool is not opened. Oh yes, there is a jacuzzi by the side for adults.

Overall, the facilities are very well maintained.

The staff are very nice and extremely helpful in our situation. We had wanted to go swimming in Singapore and remembered that a few years ago, all you had to do was tap your bus card to enter.

However, things have changed- now, in order to enter at all, you have to register for an ActiveSG account. Unfortunately for non- Singapore residents, this process is nearly impossible without a Singaporean phone number, and then also a Singaporean credit card.

Thankfully, we had helpful desk staff figure out a way to sneak through the problem, and one staff offered to use his credit card in exchange for payment in cash. We still needed to provide the postal code for our flat, but eventually, after almost half an hour of trying, we managed to get through without registering for an account.

Then, when we got inside, we realised that the entire waterslide and lazy river had been closed because of no one doing regular maintenance during Covid!

Not only that, but even though we paid for adult passes inside, the kids were not allowed into the jacuzzi because they said only adults. And the kids were not allowed on the kid's playground either because they had to be very short in order to enter.

All that said, we enjoyed swimming in the pool even though we could have went to the pool next to our flat, but we thought this would be more fun with a waterslide.

We are very dissatisfied with the system, but the only reason for 2 stars is the staff were helpful, otherwise we would have had to go home completely.
Cheap entry, normal ticket, not feature pool ticket. The big slide is quite long and the kids love it. There is also a lazy river.

Inexpensive place to spend time with family, friends or even by oneself. Big enough place with more than 4 pools move around including a huge slide. Children can play in the water playground with slides for children under 1.2m, so not much quarrel there. Olympic size indoor pool for more serious swimmers. A lazy pool just to laze around, tubes for rental available at counter.

New Revamped Jurong East Swimming Complex with Lazy and waterplay areas for the kids. Hv a good wave generation pool which operates every hour.A bit sad that the water slides has been replaced with a new children water play area for toddlers and young children. Not enough shaded canopies for users of the facility to keep their belongings the heat on hot sunny days. No food vending machines for snacks, only hv a drinks vending machine.
Fun time with kiddos. The toilets are great with large cubicles and compartments to hang your dry clothes. Definitely a comeback for my family.

Although I must say that I missed the huge slides that my generation grew up with. But it is a nice transitional change to me being a Dad now, bringing my little kiddos in what is now a kids play area.

Also the toilets have had a face-lift. Kids changing room is nice and clean. Adults changing room is equally good.

They had a baby pool, kids playground, kids splash area, lazy river and a wave pool. Wave pool had waves every hour lasting about 15mins each time. Lazy river is where we spent most of our time at, easy going and fun. Rates are affordable with activesg. Minus one star because I'd prefer having the slides.

Went with family on a Saturday. Place was busy but still have space for a good swim. Kids enjoyed the new water play area and wave pool (though wave was mild and very far apart). The lazy river is still there.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Very clean and movie quality very clear and good!👍🏻✨
Cinema here is new so come check it out while it's still very new can clean🤣🤣
Overall very nice, staffs also very friendly. Bought tickets through machine easy to use.
👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨✨👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨

my first time at shaw theatres jem! very clean and the seats were really comfortable
i was surprised by the sound system here, it’s really really good, even for a normal digital screening! out of the three times i’ve watch project hail mary this was by far the best experience
5 stars 🤩

first time coming to jem for a premium movie and it was a good experience. we only got fries but i was surprised they had ice creams and alcohol available too. the whole atmosphere is calm with dim lighting, plush seats and tons of legroom. it’s super family friendly but still feels grown up so you can actually relax. kamalii gave us a quick tour and explained all about the premium options so we knew what we were getting. the premium waiting area looks excellent and the service was really good, way better than the other outlets i’ve been to. she made us feel like vips, not just another booking. we’re definitely coming back here soon.

Stepping into the Omni-Theatre at Science Centre Singapore felt like entering a completely different world. This isn’t your usual cinema — the massive dome screen and immersive surround sound make you feel like you’re inside the movie, whether you’re flying through space or exploring nature. The visuals are incredibly sharp and engaging, and it’s a great mix of entertainment and learning for both kids and adults.
One big tip: visit on a weekday. That’s truly the best time to go. The crowd is much smaller, and it feels almost like you have the theatre to yourself, especially if you’re going with family. The experience is more relaxed, quieter, and way more enjoyable without the usual weekend rush.
Ticket prices are also very reasonable. Singaporeans and Permanent Residents pay around $8, while tourists pay about $14, which is excellent value considering how unique the experience is compared to a regular movie theatre.
What I also love is how convenient the location is. After the Omni-Theatre, you can easily spend more time nearby. KidsSTOP™ is perfect for younger children with hands-on learning and play, while Snow City is just next door if you want something fun and cooling. It’s easy to turn this into a full half-day or even full-day family outing.
Overall, the Omni-Theatre is a great place to slow down, learn something new, and enjoy quality time together. If you’re looking for something different to do in Singapore — especially on a weekday — this is definitely worth checking out.

Brought my kids here today. It was pleasant even though 20mins into the show, one fell asleep and the other one was looking forward to the “good part” as we were expecting a show about space. However it was more of a NASA showcase. Commented that she came to learn about space, not rockets 😅

Not all of the footage made use of the unique omni dome but this could be due to the film’s origin. Sound could be improved. We sat at the top near the exit. Because the back speakers were off, the show lacked impact. It was also warm as if the aircon was off.

Parking was full. Some cars had to park on the side road inside carpark. Understood from staff that this was unusual as it’s the last day of Robofest at Science Centre. Perhaps some traffic control is required for busy days such as today.

We’ll come again for other shows and hope that the management look into the above especially the show experience. Still think it’s a core memory opportunity for kids as it did for me when I visited the omni theatre back in my school days.

As part of SG60, there was a promotion to visit the science centre and the Omni theatre free.  It was a bit confusing though as although the ticket listed this two places as free, you would have to book separately for each movie slot.  I learned later that each admission ticket that day could book one movie.  However they opened up another queue for walk in admission.  And I believed anyone who used the walk in managed to go in.

I managed to watch all the films that day except for Stars Dreaming.  Animal Kingdom was the most interesting film with nice visuals and interesting facts.  Cities of the future was quite a boring one that covered a few cities around the world on the issues of environment sustainability.  Some audiences actually walked out of it.  Kitz the cat's Secret of the Space Station was the only film with a storyline and also made use of the Onimax effect to make you nauseous and dizzy in a few scenes.  It was an animation targeted at children although the theme can be quite dark.  It was still educational.  I believed it was a Korean production and some dialogues were quite awkward. Overall I would only recommend Animal Kingdom.

I found the dome screen rather dim though.  But all the pixels on the screen were working fine.  The images were not clear and the animal kingdom felt like a low Res film.  The theatre is digital 8k though according to the staff.

In terms of environment, you need to put up with talking children and eating.  Even the mother next to me kept using her hp and I need to tell her about it.  Later she started to provide narration to her child.  So be prepared to put up with the annoyances of these people.

There were a lot of stairs climbing up and down.  If you are mobility challenged you could seek help from the staff and gained access via a lift.

This theatre along with the science centre would be torn down for another new building.  Not sure would there be an Omni theatre in the future.

Long ago I watched other shows like Grand Canyon, Space walk and diving in the sea.  All were good documentaries.

Got vouchers and brought my kids (5, 10, 12 and 14yo) to watch "Ocean Paradise". The venue is very clean (no eating and drinking allowed), comfortable and has a magnificent dome screen. The movie length of 35min was perfect for my 5yo (not too long) but also not so short that the trip wasn't worth it for my 14yo. The images from this show were stunningly beautiful and the messaging was clear. Nothing scary whatsoever, which is great for my easily-scared kids, but still having a strong storyline to keep everyone engaged throughout. Volume of sounds were also not too loud (unlike movie theaters) but still clear. Do check out the highly rated Indian Food stall just outside the omnitheater, or adjourn to the Regale for some pasta (tts what we ate anyways) if spice is not an option. Bottom line: fantastic immersive movie experience for kids (and adults)!!

I think the omni is losing its touch. The documentary is very boring. People expect to feel " they are in the movie" and yet we only feel like it is looking at a documentary. A lot of people end up sleeping.
Came here for a toastmasters meeting. Great facilities and ambiance. It looks like an engaging and well operated community Center.
Thank you Veronica for helping me to settle the Singpass matters. Also Karen too for your help re the SG seniors vouchers. These are 2 beautiful, kind n patient souls!

Has a sheltered basketball court that is used for everything except basketball. Ring is missing on one side of the court.

Had so much potential as one of the only few sheltered basketball courts in the area but unfortunately poorly maintained and not used to its potential.

Can you imagine a basketball court that is booked every evening (except Tuesdays) for non-basketball related activities? This is it.

Appealing for CDC voucher, take almost 1 month didn't approve.  Call every single day,the staffs kept give the reason * in- charge is not in the office * . Just wondering when the in charge will be in the office,? And when the voucher will be issued???

I went over to do some collection and update certain issues. I was served by Mr Stanley Tan who was outstanding in his customer handling. He was patient and always answered my questions well and it showed his good product knowledge. He was courteous and a credit to your CC. Kindly convey my thanks to him

Just a road across from Jurong Point, Boon Lay Interchange and Boon Lay MRT. The Frontier CC is quite big and fully air-conditioned. There is a library, Yamaha music school and tuition/learning centers in the CC building.

Terrible long queue ever seen in frontier cc, orgainsed event "spooky night" today. The queue is extended until inside level 2 library, and the ticket exchange counter is seriously short-handed. we are queuing almost 1 hr and yet exchanged our tickets. It's not properly planned in terms of logistics this year as compared to last year. We are quite disappointed and don't think we will join next year.
Very convenient, can direct access from Jurong Point shopping mall through the flyover link. You can book the indoor sport court for badminton
Community space that combined library and services. Nice and clean place. Always go there for activities.

Cc has banned ANY types of PMA into the cc. Even a disabled person who urgently needs to use a toilet but has difficulties walking has to park OUTSIDE on the pedestrian pavement and walk in. The security guards are also very unprofessional and are against disabled people.

Forest Ramble is a fantastic children’s play wonderland nestled in nature. The themed play zones — Monkey Play, Squirrel Play, and Snake Play — are creatively designed and encourage lots of climbing, balancing, and imaginative play. It feels adventurous yet thoughtfully integrated into the surrounding greenery

The playground is well laid out with different zones catering to kids of various ages, making it easy for children to explore while adults supervise comfortably. Do note that some sections may be under maintenance at times, but there’s still plenty to keep kids entertained
A beautiful relaxing park and with nice playground. Except that some parts are closed for maintenance since 7th July 2025. That’s quite a long time for maintenance….

One of the most exciting children outdoor playground.
Plenty of different play stations.
Swings, spring jumps, slides, flying fox, mini trampoline, adventure bridges, rope runs, see saws and many many more.
Exciting moments for kids age 5 to 12 years old and 2 to 5 yrs old with adult supervision.
Shaded area, water cooler, drinks vending machine and toilets are available.
Opening hrs:
8:00am - 10:00pm,
Closed on Mon (except for PH) and Tue if preceding Mon is a PH

This was a great place to bring my nephews and cousins. The playground is spacious, fun, and has a lot of different areas to explore, so it kept everyone entertained. It’s well designed, safe, and a really nice outdoor spot for families to spend time together.

Very nice playground with lot of play setup for kids from swings to slides. Jumping climbing everything was there in the playground. On Rainy days play equipments are not allowed to use until dry. Totally recommended
New playground is such a wonderful upgrade from the old one which is so small, old and rundown.This is fun and exciting and suitable for the yonger and even older kids. Even adults.

Newly renovated playground with interesting play features like swing (that is quite rare nowadays) and a merry-go-round. The slide and climbing structure is about three storeys high, challenging enough for older kids and still manageable for slightly younger kids.
The new playground is a huge upgrade from the previous one.
Renovated and safe place for children

This particular Park Connector rans along Sungei Pandan Kechil section of West Coast Road to West Coast Highway.
It's a short 450 metre paved path.
Despite its short length, it's heavily utilised.
Residents living around the estate would walk or jog up and down this short stretch of the Connector in the morning and, in the evening.
The path would be exposed to  full sun in the early morning hrs. And seniors just love walking or do stretching exercises in the sun.

A very pretty park connector that’s perfect for running and cycling, linking West Coast Park with Clementi Woods Park. I often see people jogging here, and with the new HDB and elderly home completed, it’s even more convenient. It connects residents to nearby bus stops, the community centre, and other places easily.

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

About mid range level of difficulty. Lot of monkey though. Place is very peaceful and the lookout area is scenic. In awe. It’s connected to Rail Corridor as well, that one is pretty easy in difficulty level. It’s a literally straight road. Just need endurance for the distance.

Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve is one of the best places in Singapore to experience nature in its most authentic form. From the moment I entered via the Neo Tiew Crescent entrance, I was surrounded by lush greenery, peaceful trails, and the soothing sounds of wildlife

The reserve is exceptionally well-maintained, with clear signage, wide boardwalks, and excellent visitor facilities at the Wetland Centre. The elevated wooden walkways make it easy to explore the mangrove forests while enjoying beautiful views of the surrounding wetlands

One of the highlights of my visit was observing mudskippers hopping across the mudflats among the mangrove roots. It was fascinating to see these unique creatures thriving in their natural habitat. The reserve offers plenty of opportunities to spot wildlife, so keep an eye out for monitor lizards, crabs, and migratory birds

I was also impressed by the Mudskipper Pod, a beautifully designed wooden observation structure that blends seamlessly into the environment. It provides a cool and shaded resting point with panoramic views over the mangroves and the Johor Strait. The architecture adds a unique touch to the overall experience without detracting from the natural surroundings

A refreshing escape into nature! We enjoyed a slow and peaceful walk at Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve on May 27, fully immersed in the calm beauty of the wetlands. The walking paths were beautifully lined with lush trees, creating a naturally shaded and cooling environment that made the stroll especially comfortable even during the warmer hours.
The tranquil surroundings, gentle sounds of nature, and fresh air provided a relaxing break from the busy city atmosphere. Along the way, we appreciated the well-maintained trails and the opportunity to observe the rich biodiversity and greenery up close.
It is a wonderful place for a leisurely walk, nature appreciation, photography, or simply to unwind and recharge. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy a peaceful and scenic outdoor experience in Singapore.

A beautiful and peaceful escape into nature! The mangroves, fresh air, and rich wildlife make every walk feel special. Perfect for a relaxing stroll or bird watching—especially in the early morning or evening.
Stay curious and keep your eyes open… you might spot a giant monitor lizard crossing the path or even a crocodile resting in the water. A little adventure awaits you here!

My family took a late afternoon walk down to Sungei Buloh during low tide today to unwind, and it was a great way to clear our heads. The air had that familiar, heavy scent of salt and damp mangrove mud, and with the water stripped back, the exposed api-api roots and glistening mudflats completely changed the landscape. We spent a while just watching the giant mudskippers drag themselves across the silt and bicker over territory, which is always entertaining. The low water also meant the local birds were out in full force. We managed to spot a Great Egret freezing in place before spearing a small fish, and a Grey Heron standing completely still in the shallows waiting for Archerfish. Finishing up on the main bridge during the golden hour was the highlight, looking down into the shallow river channel at schools of Halfbeaks skimming the surface under the evening sky was incredibly peaceful and a solid reminder of how nice it is to have this kind of wilderness right on our doorstep.
Really nice nature walk. There is one bus that goes there. Can spend about 3-4 hours there. You get to see the swamps, facing JB, lizards, fishes, crabs, birds etc.

A beautiful, relaxing and vibrant place for walks, for taking a step back from the hectic pace.
Spending 3 hours walking and appreciating the fresh air, the manicured gardens, the lake and wonderful architecture, helped to destress and reset.
If you've not been, well worth some time out.

Definitely one of the nicest and must go to parks in Singapore if you are alone, couples, families group of friends wanting to escape from the concrete jungle of the city. Jurong used to be perceived as a very faraway venue to commute to but with so many new MRT train lines it’s so much easier and faster to reach. Do take note of opening hours. Free entry and easily accessible with many entrances by Chinese Garden MRT or Lakeside MRT and buses. Great for all activities, walking , strolling, jogging, cycling, picnics and there is even a skate park and dog run. What makes this park stand out from the rest is the sheer size, the large lake with canoeing; stunning replicas of historical Chinese and Japanese architecture and pagodas, bonsai garden and wildlife. And especially the stunning Lily Flower Pond at the Japanese Garden and the Lotus Flower Pond at the Chinese Garden. Definitely worth to make a trip down to enjoy this green oasis escapade rich in flora and fauna and water. It’s very tranquil and peaceful from Mondays to Saturdays. Ample toilets and drinking water dispenser and also a beverage shop selling nice cold beverages.

NParks really did a good job in revamping this park.
There's plenty of interesting plants and wildlife to spot.
Took me and my friends 3 hours to casually explore the entire area.
It's definitely a nice place to run in the mornings/evenings.

I highly recommend to visit this gardens, as there are a lot of attractions to see and they are all free! It’s a beautiful area to dive into culture and nature, just 40 min from Singapore with the bus. Very relaxing and a moment to take a pause from the overtourism and consumistic view of marina bay and the Singapore center.
Not as big as the botanic garden, but I’d say it’s better to view this than that.
Really nice gardens. Will be even more pleasant to walk in when the trees in the still new Japanese and Chinese gardens grow older and provide more shade. Looking forward to that!

Love this newly renovated Jurong Eco-Garden Park. The greenery is lush and well-maintained, with a beautiful man-made lake that adds to the peaceful atmosphere. The best time to visit is early morning or late evening. Do remember to bring sunscreen and mosquito repellent.

Be cautious of wild boars in the area, and watch your step on rocky or gravel paths, as they can be slippery or uneven. There are also some fallen branches along shaded areas that may be easy to miss, so take care while walking.

The lake is a great spot for wedding or photography shoots. You might even spot a giant turtle—likely a Southern River Terrapin—alongside common terrapins. It’s huge and almost looks like a monster! Tilapia are abundant here, and kids will enjoy feeding the fish (bring some bread).

Do note that fishing is not allowed. The toilets are clean, and there’s a water filter station available along the walking path. Overall, a lovely place to relax and enjoy nature.

Very nice garden/park! Good for casual running. Has fitness equipment and playgrounds. Has a water cooler. There are toilets and an additional toilet for individuals with limited accessibility. Lots of parking lots as well. Best timings to come are in the morning around 7-9am and in the evening 6.30-7.30pm.

A park just opposite my house, via a bridge over PIE. Quiet as little people, you can hear the birds chipping as you walk along. Playground for children, toilet available. Visit the pottery and saw lovey chickens. There are pottery for sale like huge vases, plates, cups, etc. You can walk to the campus for food, not sure if open on weekend.

It’s a very new park with lots of eco features. I’ll say that it’s actually an exhibition of water technologies used by the semi-public companies in the area and what you’re actually seeing is the experimental site where they’re showcasing the results and testing their ideas irl. Quite cool actually. BUT ALOT OF MOSQUITOES THAT WILL BITE AND STING YOU. I had itchy spots everywhere. (minus one star sorry - bugs can coexist with us but in harmony please! why the Wolbachia not working issit)

Finally the garden is open to public, nice garden for a run or cycling. There is a playground / fitness corner / toilet / drinking water and a big pond. The place is of average size. Nice little place to chill after my run.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Had a wonderful time at Ola Beach Club in Sentosa! The beachfront and poolside atmosphere makes it a perfect place to relax, unwind, and enjoy the island vibes.

A special thank you to Chef Vishnu for his warm hospitality and kind hosting. He made our visit even more enjoyable with his attentive service and welcoming attitude.

The food and drinks were great too and we especially enjoyed the pizza, refreshing juices, and delicious ice cream. Everything was fresh, tasty, and perfect for a sunny day by the beach.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for a relaxing beachside dining experience with good food, great service, and beautiful views. Looking forward to visiting again! 🌴☀️🍕🍦🥤

A nice beachside spot with a great view of the sea. The place is family-friendly and even has a pool for kids, which is a nice touch. The DJ plays good music and creates a fun atmosphere. Cocktails are okay, though they could be a bit better. Overall, a good place to relax by the beach.

Really good food and drinks. They have different plates…as share main or share starter. Drinks are really good. Also, amazing beach spot. They have a wee swimming pool if you don’t want to be on the beach. Finally, really good customer service!

My family and I have visited Ola Beach Club several times, and each visit has been truly wonderful and memorable. The food and drinks are consistently excellent, and the ambiance strikes the perfect balance between relaxing and fun.

It’s a fantastic place for both the young and the young at heart to unwind, bond, and spend quality time together. Friends can also enjoy meaningful catch-ups in a vibrant yet laid-back setting.

For the kids, the swimming pool, sand play area, and banana boat ride are definite highlights, making the experience even more enjoyable for the whole family. Totally recommend this place!
Food were pretty good, we enjoyed our food! The pacing of the dishes could be better, my children have almost finished their food before ours were served. We enjoyed the atmosphere and the service!

Met an old friend here. The place looks nice. We lost in touch soon after. Pity
viewed two units. location not bad, but the workmanship is terrible. it is ridiculous that a 5 years condo has peeling tiles everywhere and broken sewage filter here and there.

It is a cosy place to stay. Ambience of a resort. Without telling you more, you will be thinking as if you are staying in resorts such as  Phuket, Bali, Pattaya or even Seychelles. Reasonable size function room with nice blueish swimming pool coupled with nice beautiful clouds. The units of each apartments ceiling is about 3m high. Behind it has a big n nice public park. Built n designed by World Class but minimum it a class of its uniqueness. Hard to come by with nice and big landscape. Nowadays it is difficult to find a condominiums with such a big and scenic landscape.

A transition between new and old HDB flats of about 30 years in Teck Whye Estate.
