Quiet and simple straight path

We’ve been so happy with our girls’ experience at LFP West Coast! The teachers put a lot of thought and effort into creating engaging programmes that the children truly enjoy. Every day, our girls come home excited to share what they’ve learned. It’s clear they feel confident and happy in school.

We also really appreciate the school’s strong parent engagement with regular updates, thoughtful communication, and opportunities to be part of the learning journey. The fees are reasonable for the quality of care and education provided, making it great value for money.

Highly recommend the school to any parent looking for a nurturing, enriching environment where children can thrive!

Both my boys are enrolled in LFP@West Coast. Teachers are friendly and dedicated in their roles, many times going over and above. Kids enjoy school alot. Curriculum is good and my p1 going kid has transited well to primary school. Highly recommended!

We are so grateful to have found this childcare centre! All my 4 kids are with this preschool! The teachers are incredibly caring, patient and professional — they treat every child like their own and always create a warm, nurturing environment. Our child has grown so much in confidence, independence and language ever since joining. The activities are creative and well-structured, and we love the regular photo updates that let us see what our little one is learning each day. The centre is clean, safe and very well-organised. Highly recommend to any parent looking for a loving and supportive place for their child to grow and thrive! ❤️

Due to the closing of the previous preschool last year, I frantically needed to find a new school for my son. Was recommended to this school which is not too far away from home.

Boy was happy since day 1. Even though we will only be here for 1 year, he has grown more mature and more responsible and look forward to going to school every day. I am sure the teachers and staff (and the kids!) have welcomed him with open arms.

Thank you to Mrs Teoh, the teachers and staff for making his final year of preschool memorable.

P.S. Even though I am only familiar with his form teachers, the other teachers also shared with me what they observed and feedback to me during pick up. Appreciate it!

My daughter has just joined PG early this year (at 25 months), and the teachers have been so patient with her - to get her comfortable, to correct her actions and helped her integrate into the class.

She has been enjoying going to school! She communicates better, eat well and get to socialize more with her other peers and teachers.

Happy to have gotten off the waitlist and have Little Footprints as my daughter’s first school
It's a great student care center. My child is very happy there because of the good playmates and classmates, as well as the enthusiastic and responsible teachers.

Graceland Student Care has been a warm and caring environment for our child. The teachers are friendly, patient, and genuinely attentive to the children’s well-being.

We’re truly thankful for the dedication and kindness shown by the team.

I wanted to take a moment to express my gratitude for the wonderful experience my child has had at Graceland Student Care Centre.

From the moment we enrolled our child at your center, we were impressed by the emphasis placed on creating a safe and secure environment. The center's commitment to maintaining a clean and well-maintained facility, coupled with the stringent safety measures in place, reassured us as parents that our child was in good hands.

The teachers and caregivers have consistently gone above and beyond to create a nurturing and warm atmosphere for the children. Their patience, compassion, and attentiveness have fostered a sense of belonging and security for my child. It is heartwarming to witness the genuine connections they have formed with the children, making them feel valued and loved.

The open and effective communication between the center and parents has been outstanding. The regular updates on my child's progress, along with the opportunity to discuss any concerns or queries, have greatly contributed to my peace of mind. The staff's willingness to listen, address our questions, and collaborate on my child's development has been invaluable.

In conclusion, I wholeheartedly recommend Graceland Student Care Center to any parent seeking a student care center that provides a safe, nurturing, and supportive environment. The exceptional care, dedicated staff, and commitment to holistic development have truly made a positive impact on my child's growth and happiness. Thank you for all that you do!

I am particularly grateful for the additional academic support provided by the center. The teachers are always available to provide guidance, clarify concepts, and assist with homework assignments. They create a nurturing and encouraging environment where my child feels comfortable asking questions and seeking help. The feedback and constructive guidance they offer have been invaluable in my child's academic progress.

Overall, I am extremely pleased with my child's experience at Graceland Student Care Center. I highly recommend this center to any parent seeking quality care and educational support for their child.

Firstly, the teachers here are very professional and will be able to help and explain to me any questions no matter what the subject is. Secondly, this place have holiday activities daily and always have at least one excursion during the holidays. All of this keeps me excited and I will always look forward to coming here again. Thirdly, Graceland Student Care is very near to schools such as Rulang Primary and Shuqun Primary. My kindergarten friends there who also came here and said that their experience here is very memorable and unforgettable! Lastly, this Students Care Centre will also provide fetching form Rulang Primary and Shuqun Primary and so the parents will not have to worry about their child journey to Graceland Student Care. And just by coming here, I am able to make many friends form different schools.
If you are looking for a student care in Jurong, I will definitely recommend you to come here!
Hope that your children will have a good time here!

After 15 years, NJC's boarding school (only) was officially closed in 2024 to return the land for state use.

These images hope to honour the memories of those 500 students and teachers boarded there every year

For more at #shuutur

Very disappointing. The secondary level teachers dont really teach. I was expecting some principles to be taught in class so I kept quiet and observed my kid's progress. Wasn't taught. Disappointed. They also got 1 alumni to teach while waiting for her A results. Teachers not teaching in way that can be understood. The school relies on private tuition and hard self study. A few teachers talks disrespectful like are sarcastic. Student prefers the previous principal

One of the famous schools in Singapore and undoubtedly with lots of heritage, history and culture. As you step into the school you feel the nostalgia and history working together to build a wonderful, hopeful and resilient new generation one after the other.

Shoes and shoelaces that are garish in nature are not allowed. W school, W people, W teachers, and most importantly, W chillies, popcorn and math.

Everyday I go to school and I look forward to seeing my math work. The piles and piles of math homework does not do itself. It sure is great experience and practice!!!

The accidental cohesive nature of this school is truly great. All that gleams is not gold. The equal funding given to the aesthetics and science departments are truly interesting.

I recall the days I went to NJC. Oh my, it has been quite a bit... I can recall the days we got our exam results back. I remember the smiles our teacher tried to hide from the students when they gave us those earth-shatteringly wonderful results. They smiled so wide, so wide we could barely see the tears forming in their eyes as they looked at our shattered expressions in pride.

The teachers love teaching, sometimes a little too much. I see their beautiful teary-eyes smiles upon their weary faces whenever they step into class. I see it, I feel it, I know it, it is ingrained into each and every one of their fabric of reality. As an alumni of this school, I missed the days of boarding. The chaotic thwacking of doors whenever we closed a door was truly inspiring.

The myths are not true. We do not have a leaky roof. In fact, it is the best roofs of all. The roof's whole is about winning. In fact, it is so winnable that it has always allowed water through it. It has never lost its purpose in letting water through.

One of the key problems today is that math is undervalued in this society, and that there is too much emphasis on the pursuit of arts. What even is the use for arts? Useless pining? Useless uselessness? Useless emotions? None of these things are needed. Join NJC, for you will see the world for what it is, through good, or through better, or through best, and that, is through math.

Our integrated programme is good. No, the best at INTEGRATING!!!! Our streak of streaky teary students crying tears of joy at the sight of their beautiful eggs received on their math papers is unmatched. One must join this institution to truly understand the meaning of life. For it is to MATH.

I truly apologise for this accidental cohesive multicultural heritage-ridden serviced and honoured post. NJCians truly deserve more math homework.

Teachers are unable to teach. You need to engage tutors to get your child to understand the material. They overload the students with homework and you pray that your child survives this barrage of tests, the humiliation of failing and the lack of proper help to improve. Don't send your child here unless he/she is prepared to weather the storm and you are prepared for the many teacher/parent sessions.

Very clean and well maintained. I went there for a seminar on 30th May about Machining Learning and AI at NUS School of Computing.

The event was held at COM 1 building and was well organised by both NUS and EMERITUS.

Besides SOC, I visited NUH and NUS School of Design and Engineering as well and was impressed by its cleanliness.

Can’t wait to possibly start a postgraduate program here 😶‍🌫️

Had a great experience today at NUS Open House 2023! Although everything was only taking place at UTown, it was an extremely vibrant and enjoyable experience. There were so many activities and booths around, and it was such fun and pleasure to be at the place!

Thoroughly enjoyed my 1-day outing to the campus, where students and staff alike are very welcoming, friendly, and awesome! Thank you NUS :)

P.S. it’s a great time to visit on weekends just to walk around and admire the architecture of the campus!

Your application website is trash. I don't know who wrote your website but the person did not pass computer science for sure. Your website logs out every 30 minutes and the problem is you don't save the progress that students have made. In the end they have to refill everything again and you know how long it takes to do that. You better solve that asap otherwise you will lose  amazing students.

Amazing place for students worldwide. Top notch facilities for everything you care about. really interesting lot of students to hang around. You'll love this space , the Campus is attracted all over the city of Singapore. For vegetarians, it's a bit tricky to get the desired food. Amazing public transport and AQI less than 50.

I was amazed when I visited this campus back in 2014. Still I'm wondering how an university campus can be so beautiful. This campus is the combination of the any modern shopping mall (e.g. vibocity) and a decent forest. One can enjoyed these both feeling once he/she inside of it. Everything is heavily organized and the campus is so bug that you can not visit the entire campus in a single day. The campus is full of stairs and that's why sometimes it is called national university of Stairs.

SUSS has been a meaningful part of my academic journey, especially since it was the only institution that accepted my application. While the curriculum is undeniably rigorous, it has also pushed me to grow intellectually and adapt to increasingly complex expectations, particularly as I approach Year 3. Some assignments are quite challenging and require a high level of critical thinking, which can be demanding but ultimately rewarding.

One concern, however, is the school’s efforts to combat the use of AI. While the intention is understandable, the system has flagged a significant number of false positives, creating unnecessary stress and anxiety among students. This has, at times, affected the overall learning experience and assignments submission. I hope that the institution can fine tune and resolve this issue soon.

Additionally, certain components such as the e-portfolio—especially in the last semester—feel like an added chores for students. Instead of adding meaningful value to learning, it can come across as repetitive and disconnected from more practical or relevant academic outcomes. Suss might want to review if Eportfolio is truly necessary.

Despite these challenges, SUSS remains a good institution that offers a strong platform for preparing students for the realities of the working world. Its emphasis on applied learning and resilience equips students with practical skills that extend beyond the classroom.

SUSS, some jokingly called it Singapore University of Self-Study due to its nature of promoting adult learners who are capable of doing their own independent learning to improve classroom discussions, are also known as the Singapore University of Stairs and Slopes now due to their recent upgrades by me. I used to not find the trail to classroom not so physically challenging until recently, maybe it’s just me :)

The Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS), vibrantly anchored at 463 Clementi Road, is a truly magnificent phenomenon in the educational landscape that I find deeply inspiring for its unwavering commitment to "Head, Heart, and Habit." Having engaged in numerous enlightening dialogues with passionate social workers and empathetic human resource leaders in my network, I am genuinely fascinated by how SUSS operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where academic rigor is inextricably linked to community impact. My acquaintances often emphasize that the pedagogical energy within its Clementi campus is exceptionally inclusive, offering a rare environment where lifelong learners and young undergraduates alike are empowered to master complex counseling or supply chain systems under the guidance of socially-conscious mentors. Public sentiment remains remarkably high, viewing the university not merely as an adult learning center, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in the 2026 silver economy and non-profit sectors. Reflecting on these recurring, authentic testimonials from peers who have transformed their local neighborhoods, I perceive this institution as a distinguished powerhouse of human progress that executes its mission with a level of humanitarian audacity and Singaporean pragmatism that is truly refreshing. This academic landmark is consistently described by those "in the know" as a place where the theoretical sparks of social theory are transformed into the tangible steel of community resilience. Furthermore, the university's pioneering focus on applied learning ensures that graduates enter the labor market as highly specialized, compassionate assets with a distinct ethical edge. I personally admire the way SUSS fosters a culture of mutual respect and social responsibility, treating every student as a future steward of a more equitable society. The campus itself is often praised for its accessible, collaborative atmosphere, providing a professional backdrop that mirrors the inclusive environments our future social pioneers are destined to lead. It is heartening to hear from my network that the university’s flexibility is not just a logistical feature, but a core philosophy that respects the diverse life paths of its students. Many of my colleagues point out that SUSS’s industry-integrated projects provide an invaluable bridge to the real world, ensuring that theoretical knowledge never stays trapped in a book. I find it remarkable how the institution maintains its status as a premier public university while remaining so grounded in the everyday needs of the people. Every conversation I engage in regarding this university reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and lifelong empowerment. The school’s dedication to the "Humanities for the 21st Century" is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for an education that values people over mere profit. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with the Singapore University of Social Sciences is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to navigate the complexities of the modern world while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to the power of education when it is fueled by a genuine desire to serve the greater good.

School admins are mostly very unhelpful. Any issue made over calls are often handled poorly by people who never know how to direct me to the right person and try to handle it themselves. Finding the right person to communicate with is sometimes difficult because of this.

School rarely takes responsibility for their actions and often shoves responsibility onto students. They will hardly put in effort to try and properly understand any issues you have
If "standard procedures" solutions does not help solve your issue then that is the extent of their help they bother to offer. They even suggest solutions which clearly doesnt help solve the issue at hand and sometimes completely irrevelvent to the issue due to misunderstanding the issue.

would not recommend enrolling here.

Excellent teachers, teachers are friendly, understanding and patient. During my O levels, I was failing at maths and chemistry, but after signing up for this tuition and recieving the necessary and appropriate guidance, I was able to go from a D or F, to a B and higher - In the span of 8 months and with one lesson roughly weekly. I strongly believe that the teachers provide a conducive and comfortable learning environment that allows students to feel more at ease whilst learning. The teachers will go the extra mile, literally as they will make the effort to travel far distances in order to teach in an area that is closer to the student's homes for their convenience, however this is most commonly when there are multiple students in that area. I find that many students will enjoy the learning process and perform better after applying for this tuition as long as they are willing to learn.

Tuition@Pioneer has been an amazing help! The teachers are patient, encouraging, and really take the time to make sure every student understands the concepts. My confidence and results improved so much thanks to them. Highly recommend to anyone looking for quality guidance and a supportive learning environment!

Peng Rong was a really kind and helpful tutor. He always made time to hold classes even though he had a very busy teaching schedule. The lessons held were all very  helpful and if you are willing to do well with tuition trust me, you would want him as your tutor 👍
Peng Rong is an awesome teacher, he is kind and caring for students
Peng Rong was a really caring and patient teacher. Would recommend

Internet, chatgbt, google, Ai and the pervasive mobile devices may have changed the way we seek answers. But a library is where serious learnings and discoveries are found.

They are depository of knowledge, curated and preserved. The Central Library is that - and more.

There are librarians dedicated to the preservation of knowledge and their proliferation. Here, they are not stuffy and grumpy matrons. They are professionals and helpful in a way we boomers never knew.

Tell them a hypothesis and they can help you turn an idea into blueprint. Need to fill a gap in citations, they can find you a landfill.

History is our heritage. Lessons learned to inform us. Latest technology is flashy but libraries tell us how they came about.

The Central Library is more than a meeting place for varsity students. Here is where a thousand nuggets of wisdom and knowledge exists - waiting for those with inquiring minds to be transformed into “professors.”

lots of study spots! most of the library is for nus students/staff only as there are gantries at the entrance which mainly open with student/staff passes

most study spots have charging spots, each level has a bathroom and water dispensers. there's also food options at level 1 and a bookstore with essentials

only complaint is that the wifi can be quite slow/spotty at times. also level 6 study area can get quite warm sometimes when its busy, like the ac isnt strong enough :")))

I spend a lot of time in this library when I did my undergrad and post grad studies. It has many books which were not available in other libraries. I did join as a corporate member for a few years too.
Beautiful and modern library with a very rich collection of books and ample seating space.

I entered as an alumnus. I needed an NUS alumnus lifelong email address and also had to download and install the UNivUS app, which generates the QR code for me to enter the library. There was mobile data signal at the registration counter therefore I needed to log into NUG Guest wifi. But the NUS Guest wifi needed an OTP which required mobile signal! Therefore I needed to go back and forth between the Forum (mobile signal to get OTP) and library registration desk (for wifi). Altogether, it took me perhaps half an hour to sort it a out and finally enter the library. Future trips should be straightforward I hope!!! General stacks on 5th floor. Photocopier is on 5th floor but in the Chinese SEA Collection. Use your EZ-link card. $0.03 per A4 - great value!  :-)  The facilities are lovely -wow... compared to 1990s! Love the place. The Coop had lots of cute merch too. Ah... to be a Gen Z or Gen Alpha... 😄😄😄

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

Clean MRT. Quiet on weekends. Little crowded during peak hour on weekdays.

Would like to give much thanks to Charles for ensuring my safety and being a lovely person. I was on the last train and overshot my stop, ending up at Gulf Circle past midnight without a train back, so I asked the service counter where to find the bus stop.  Charles stepped out however and accompanied me to the stop, even waiting for me to board the bus. He did so as he knew how dangerous it would be for a young girl like me at night. I felt extremely safe that night and grateful for his accompaniment. Thanks Charles and all the best to you!

Opened on 18 June 2017 as part of the Tuas West Extension (TWE) of the East–West MRT Line (EWL), the station is located at the boundaries of Gul Circle and Tuas North planning subzones at Tuas Road near the junction with Gul Circle and Tuas Avenue 3. The station is named after the nearby road of the same name which serves the Gul industrial area including the industries in the vicinity of Tuas Road such as Tuas Avenue 3, Tuas Avenue 5, Gul Circle and Gul Crescent. The station features a Cheers store which is located at Level 2 of the station and is served by SBS Transit bus services 192 and 193 which run between Tuas Bus Terminal and Boon Lay Bus Interchange as well as Transtar Travel cross-border bus service TS6 which runs between The Star Vista at Buona Vista and GP Sentral Bus Terminal in Gelang Patah, Johor Bahru, Malaysia. In addition, there is a single taxi stand and two pick-up and drop-off points, one of which is outside exit A of the station along Tuas Road before the junction with Gul Circle near JTC Space @ Gul and the other one is outside exit B of the station along Tuas Avenue 3 before the junction of Tuas Road near Airtrade Systems Pte Ltd.
Bus stop smelly
Good

Tong luck cai fan
Damn expensive. This is tuas industrial area.. More ex than heartland coffeeshop. One veg one meat one egg =$5. 10

I visited this Indian food stall this morning and ordered a dosa. I wanted to try both the sambar and chutney, so I asked for an extra small bowl to keep them separate. The vendor suggested I pour the curry directly onto the dosa to avoid using an extra plate, but I didn’t want my dosa to get soggy, so I kindly asked for the bowl.

Unfortunately, the vendor seemed frustrated and questioned why I needed so much for just one dosa, saying, "Don’t ask too much next time." While I understand the effort to reduce waste, I felt the vendor could have been more understanding of my personal preferences as a customer.

The food was good, but the interaction left me feeling a little uncomfortable. Hopefully, in the future, they can be more open to accommodating different customer requests.
Yummy delicious vegetarian food found here...can't go wrong even for non-veg eater.
Non air conditioning food court. Busy during normal working days during lunch hour. Offers 1 malay and Indian food stall besides others. Reasonable prices.
Food stalls not that many. Alot of stalls are vacant. Food wise still ok to eat.
Place wise not bad, so far Malay, Indian, this Chinese and another roasted chicken rice stall are good. Long Q if you are after 1130am but still plenty of seats. Down side is not much parking lots.

Limited parking lots, so either go early at 11am or just swing by and get takeout. Great variety of food at great prices.
Chinese chicken rice stall has delicious curry noodles as well in addition to roast meats. Malay stall (1st stall when you walk in from the car park) has a wide variety of food, from rendang to ayam Merah to sotong and stingray depending on the day and your luck.

A great place to go for food in this godforesaken place called Gul.
Had my pack lunch from Authentic Nasi Padang, Lots of dishes to choose from. Nice and it's value for money

Short of parking lots in morning peak hour. To worsen, some inconsiderate Motorcyclists any how park at limited car park lots eg.1 or 2 bikes occupied 1 full Cp lot. There was no deliberate monitoring and control observed. See picture taken 18 July 2018 which is a common scenario every morning. Stalls food 🍲 overall are best and price competitive. Wide variety of food type. The am porridge by 2 stalls best of best
Xing Xing mixed curry rice very expensive. 3 ingredients charge $5. Won’t buy from this store again.

The ice cream and waffles are tasty! I had cookies monster and waffles. The service was also commendable. Good neighbourhood location too. Nice place for friends to hangout.Thanks to Yuai and Yuan Sheng for accommodating to my request. Cheers!

really enjoyed their gelato and waffles! the waffles were crisp at the edges but had a chewy centre, which made every bite really enjoyable 😋 the hojicha gelato had a strong tea fragrance without being too sweet, while the pistachio one was another good and safe choice.

for a sweet & savoury combination, you can even get their mentaiko tater tots that were freshly fried and went perfectly with the umami mentaiko sauce 🍟🥔

Crispy, golden waffle topped with a scoop of creamy ice cream—simple, delicious, and perfectly balanced. Warm meets cold in the best way. Definitely a must-try.

Great waffle café with a cozy vibe. Friendly service, and fair prices. Definitely worth a visit!
Great friendly and fast service. The waffle is nice but abit dry today. Anyways hoping for paddlepop flavour!!
really amazing one of a kind waffles and unique ice cream flavours and super conducive to chill or study  will come back again! 🍨

Newly opened Grounds on a Hill at Sunset Way is a clean, minimal, coffee-first space. Bright and airy inside, with the smell of fresh coffee carrying through the room. Outdoor seating is breezy and comfortable, anchored by solid wood block benches. Menu is simple with items like sourdough bread, which I didn't sample this visit.

Both white and black coffees were excellent. The house blend leans fruity with gentle berry notes, well-balanced and not overly acidic.

this was my second time at grounds, I had a lovely chat over carefully made coffee, with smiles and a warm welcome by the team. I learnt that this was a family run business, with beans roasted fresh themselves.

this abode in sunset way provides that human touch we need, and a place to really rest and escape for a little!

Very chill cafe that is located further inside from the main road (though the area is relatively small anyways) - but very well worth the walk there as they do serve up great coffee in a cool minimalistic environment!

☕ another coffee that hits the benchmark, the plum flavour really did follow through in my iced white, and similarly for the hot drink as well - not the best of the best but that’s my benchmark for a 5 stars coffee, being able to taste the aroma from my drink

🍞 the sourdough was alright, pretty pricey for the portion though it was a decent snack to be had

Might return to try their tarts in future, and also their other beans too!

Excellent coffee in a very well-designed coffee space, that is dog friendly. It’s one of my favourite places to bring people to. Thrilled to see adventurous coffee businesses bringing specialty coffee to the heartlands and neighbourhoods. Great enterprise by Rio and his daughters Paris and Sydney.

So pleased to have found this coffee shop, a real treasure in the most unassuming of locations.

The philosophy is simple; fantastic coffee at affordable prices. The family that own and run this place have an endearing love of the coffee they make, and they keep it simple - two varieties of filter coffee (the Peruvian Geisha is phenomenal!) and two varieties of espresso, all roasted to suit. You can see the passion that goes into each cup that is carefully & beautifully crafted. A simple but quality food menu as well and that’s it!

There is relaxed indoor and outdoor seating and I all-too-easily managed to pass a couple of hours there with a good book. If you’re in Singapore and you love speciality coffee, don’t miss it!

Honestly taste like home cooked food, fish curry is must try , fresh veggies , roti prata is so good and tasty.
Must try and highly recommended.
Awesome food at affordable price...must try in Singapore

For a $5.50 bak kut teh, they gave generous 3 big pieces of pork rib and hearty soup. Strong recommendation.

In fact, I loved it so much that I helped them to create a separate Google Maps location so that they can appear separate from the Joo Koon eating house where this store is located.

Lunch time will always be crowded, genuine hidden gem.
Delicious soup.

place_name: Twenty Eight Cafe+ Kopi 1930 National Gallery
review_text:
Place gets crowded and you choose your own table and order from the QR code. Food is priced on the higher side - but taste wise doesn’t really match the price. It’s around 20-30 for the western dishes and about 10-15 for the kopitiam local dishes.

Seafood aglio olio 3/5 :

Generous amount of lala clams and prawns, and seafood was very fresh. Aglio olio wise could have been more flavourful for the pasta and was a little soggy.

Kaya soufflé pancake 2/5 :

Delicious homemade kaya and crispy bread crackers on the side, as well as delicious salted cream and coconut flakes on the pancake, just that the pancake was not jiggly enough and too solid, resulting in a pretty cakey mouthfeel and texture instead of a melt in your mouth feeling.

Pesto pasta:

Creamy, sauce coated the mafalde pasta well. Could use a little more pesto flavour imo!

Ayam berempah thigh 2/5:

Could have been more flavourful, strips cut too thin so they taste dry and stringy when fried

Tater tots w mentaiko sauce 2/5:

Could have given more mentaiko sauce for the amount of taters given, taters has a slightly over oily taste (I get that it’s deep fried but it was a little too gelat still)

Bee Hoon with luncheon meat and Ngoh Hiang 3/5 :

Was $9, a little pricey.

Ribena fizz 3/5:

$5, tastes like it sounds.

place_name: Twenty Eight Cafe+ Kopi 1930 National Gallery
review_text:
🟨 Twenty Eight Cafe  ( 🩷 new)
1 Saint Andrew's Rd, #01-17
National Gallery, Singapore 178957

🟨 Location
***************
🟨 Visited this newly-opened cafe at The National Gallery with a friend. 🟨 The cafe is located on ground floor.

🟨 Once you catch sight of the museum gift shop ➖️ you turn to the right, and the cafe is there.
_____________________________________
🔵 Ambience
*****************
🔵 The cafe has 2 dining concepts. 🔵 One side of the cafe is occupied by Kopi 1930, which serves local Singapore dishes and local beverages.

🔵  The 2nd portion of the space features "Twenty Eight" cafe ➖️ which is a western style concept ( ➡️ serving western style brunch items and such).

🟥 For these 2 cafe concepts, no pork and no lard is used in all their dishes.

🔵  Adjacent to the long glass counter are more seats available. ●Over at this end, the cafe space stretches over a long dining hall area. ▪︎The interior decor palette is a soothing mix of white, black, grey, cream, brown and dark green hues.

🔵 There are window nook seats on one side of the cafe. ● These cosy window box seats offer a quiet sense of privacy, with natural day-light streaming in through the large vintage style latticed windows.
_____________________________________

🟢 Food
**************
🟢 My friend and I ordered 4 items:-
***************************************
🟩  2 Mugs of Teh-C (➖️ ie local tea with Carnation Milk priced at SGD3.20 per cup).

🟩 Kaya & Butter on Crackers / SGD2.50 for 2 pieces.

🟢 Chicken Thigh Berempah / SGD8.90 ( side dish)

🟢 All items were well crafted. The Teh-C was rich with the creaminess of the evaporated milk and had the depth and flavour of well brewed local tea notes.

🟢 I love the Chicken Thigh Berempah dish. The chicken pieces were well-marinated with local spices and were served hot. They were crispy, succulent and featured unami flavours.

🟢 The Kaya & Butter Crackers brought a sense of nostalgia to me. Because this lovely heritage snack is what I used to eat during my childhood days in the 1970s.
_______________________________________
🟪  Service = The staff at the order station ( cashier's counter) were all friendly, helpful and affable. I also met Ms Cynthia Lim, the owner of the cafe.

🟪 She had a most pleasant and kind demeanour and was amiable and very nice to talk to. We had a short but interesting chat about the new cafe's star dish items.

🟪 I was informed by Ms Cynthia that their other 2 outlets at Wilkie Road area ( ➖️ also named Kopi 1930 & Twenty Eight cafe) features different signature star dishes.
________________________________________
✳️ Overall, this newly-opened cafe is a lovely spot to take a short break.  ● Am impressed with the level of service here and I wish to thank Ms Cynthia and her crew members for their omotenashi spirit of hospitality.

❇️ Monday / 3 Nov 2025

place_name: Twenty Eight Cafe+ Kopi 1930 National Gallery
review_text: I really enjoyed the kopitiam-style dishes here the flavors were authentic and satisfying. The ambiance, especially with the beautiful setting alongside the National Gallery Singapore, made the experience even more special. The service was attentive and pleasant as well. It was my first visit, and it definitely exceeded expectations ,well worth it!

place_name: Twenty Eight Cafe+ Kopi 1930 National Gallery
review_text: We had a lovely lunch here with my child. The food was delicious, and the wait time was pleasantly short. Service was excellent, with attentive and friendly staff throughout. The atmosphere was bright and welcoming on a sunny afternoon, and our window seats overlooking the Padang made the experience even more special.

place_name: Twenty Eight Cafe+ Kopi 1930 National Gallery
review_text: Very cosy place at the National Gallery to have a relaxing meal. Toast sets were OK.

korean food stall that sells tasty korean food dishes. ingredients used were fresh, portion large and very reasonably priced. must try BBQ spicy chicken rice set and bibimbap. homemade iced lemon tea is good too!
First time visiting. Well ventilated and litted canteen in tuas area. Variety of food is available, muslim, non-muslim and  vegetarian. Washroom available on site. Car Parks available at roadside.

Today morning, I went to the food court again and decided to pack lunch from the Chinese non-vegetarian mix rice stall. I requested for the side dishes to be packed separately in plastic and the rice in polystyrene, because mixing everything together causes the food to spoil.

To my shock, the vendor flatly refused, even when I offered to pay for the extra plastic. This came across as extremely stingy and cheapskate. As a paying customer, I find it unacceptable that such a basic and reasonable request was turned down, especially when I was willing to bear the cost.

It is disappointing to see this kind of rigid and inconsiderate service. I hope management looks into this matter seriously, as this attitude reflects very poorly on the food court’s standards and customer care.
The noodles comes in quite a large portion. The soup was a little oily because of the fried garlic put inside. The coffee here needs improvement.

Claypot curry chicken is very tasty, servicing is big (one full chicken drumstick), uncle auntie are very friendly as well.
Must try!

Jurong Point has really evolved into one of the largest and most comprehensive suburban malls in Singapore over the years. The mall keeps expanding and now offers an impressive variety of restaurants, cafés, retail shops, supermarkets, and leisure options all under one roof. There are also at least two sizeable food courts with plenty of local and international food choices, making it easy to find something for everyone

One of the biggest advantages is the excellent convenience - it has always been directly connected to both the MRT station and the bus interchange, making it extremely accessible rain or shine. Despite being a heartland mall, it feels vibrant and lively with many different sections to explore, from Japanese-themed food streets to family-friendly spaces and lifestyle shops

Jurong Point is one of the more convenient suburban malls in Singapore with a huge variety of shops, supermarkets, restaurants and daily necessities all under one roof. The mall is directly connected to Boon Lay MRT, so accessibility is definitely a big plus.

What I like most is the wide food selection ranging from local hawker-style eateries to proper restaurants and cafes. It’s also spacious enough for long walks without feeling too cramped on weekdays.

However, the mall can get extremely crowded during weekends and peak dining hours, especially around the food areas. Some sections also feel slightly dated compared to newer malls in Singapore, and navigating between the different wings can be a bit confusing initially.

Overall, still a very practical and convenient mall for shopping, dining and everyday errands in the west side of Singapore.

As one of Singapore’s largest suburban malls, Jurong Point serves as a massive retail and lifestyle hub for the West. It’s famous for its immersive themed precincts and endless food options, making it a hotspot for both locals and visitors.
For my family and me living in the West, Jurong Point is our go-to spot for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient. Plus, being directly connected to Boon Lay MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

Didn’t expect tiramisu to hit this good, but here we are at Tirato Tiramisu location at B1 near boast.

Caved and tried two flavours — pistachio and yam — and honestly? No regrets. Just me wondering why I didn’t get more

Pistachio was rich, nutty, and somehow still light? Super creamy, but not the kind that makes you feel jelak. The subtle crunch inside? Chef’s kiss. Definitely feels a little luxe.

Yam caught me off guard — it’s soft, naturally sweet, and has that nostalgic vibe, like something you grew up eating but upgraded. Super comforting and lowkey addictive.

What made it even better:
✨ No pork, no lard, no alcohol — totally kid-friendly (my little cousin loved it)
✨ Freshly made daily — no preservatives, no colouring, nothing artificial
✨ So many flavours to choose from — I was stuck at the counter for 5 mins trying to pick. Spoilt for choice fr.

They’re only $6.80 each, which feels like a steal for the quality.
Very fun shopping at Jurong point. Lots of things to see for Chinese new year from ornaments to cookies in jars and gift boxes. Got god of fortune everywhere too. Pineapple tarts and more ~

Since moving back to the west from the east, Sheng Siong has become one of my usual supermarkets that I frequently get my groceries from. The supermarket is spacious with a wide variety of selections, and everything is always neatly arranged. The vegetables and fruits are fresh as well.

The staff there are also very friendly, especially two ladies whom I encountered.  Darchana and Santos are very helpful and professional. Darchana always smiley and very helpful whenever assistance is needed. Small gestures like these really make the shopping experience more comfortable and pleasant.

I was in a rush so I didn’t manage to take more pictures, but I’ll update with more photos the next round.

Cashiers are fast and friendly. Will remind customers if they are Chas or Pioneer on the designated days.

The 2 storey Sheng Siong is 24 hours. Appreciate the long operating hours.

Peaceful shopping can be done after mid night.

Early in the morning, there's staff to help butcher pork to ur desire. Choose a piece of meat and they minced for u. Pork Belly if not much people, they can slice it for u.

Fish counters operate till later timing then pork. Large selection of prawns, fish, crabs.

Outdoor area, there's plant section, veg section and promotion section.

Variety is huge!

Efficient, tidy, with enormous range of food, groceries, household appliances and sanitary products that you might not find in normal grocery stores.

The clean and well lit enterprise also keeps jaywalkers and suspicious people away, there used to be provocatively dressed women harassing motorcyclists for cigarettes years ago. They and an ever rising count of shoplifters have been dealt with swiftly by the authorities.

It helps that the staff are very friendly and welcoming to all cultures and preserve the Singapore spirit of orderly and honest business.

24-hour supermarket at West with a wide range of food & many varieties. Never worry about being unable to buy fresh seafood cod this outlet 24 hours yet can find almost everything we need. From food to household items, too

I love supermarkets, especially the bigger ones. I miss the old Sheng Siong Supermarket at Tekka Mall, which had unfortunately closed years ago.
This outlet seems rather big to me; Spacious with a great selection of affordable daily essentials. A really good place for grocery shopping.

** 4% special discount for seniors (Singaporeans/ PRs) 60 and above, every Tuesday and Wednesday.
**The Tem Tem Protein Tempeh Chips sold here are suitable for vegetarians, healthy and super delicious. Get yours now for the coming Chinese New Year. You’re worth pampering.

The convenience store is located just outside the MRT gantry, making it very accessible.
The store has a decent variety of products.
I was delighted to find the product I wanted for lunch. The convenience store is neat and well organised. I was able to find the chicken meat easily.

Bought 2 Americano coffee and Haribo sweets here today. All was good and tasty products
Just one thing. I used Yuu app to redeem points as payment, but wrong amount was deducted by seller person. I returned back and after short discussion got back the difference in cash
I interacted with one of the staff members an Indian lady who, unfortunately, came across as unfriendly and appeared to lack knowledge about the products in the store.
31 Dec 2025 6pm. Indian lady cashier keep talking on phone and not being helpful.

I went there to buy a gift card the gift card was $30 and I had $60 so I decided to buy 2 and on the gift card it says that the gift card will only be activated at the cashier, when I pay, the cashier use one of the gift card to scan she use it to scan two times but she didn’t scan the another one when I when type in the code in my phone I realise that one of the gift card did not work and on my phone it said gift card not activated so I decided to go BACK to the Joo Koon mrt 7/11 and ask the staff to help me activate it , i also told her why the card did not work and I also showed her the receipt and also explained why this happened,I explained everything in English then she ask me to speak in Chinese……….    And when I was explaining it in English ,there was people waiting behind me at I wanted to just give up and go but she didn’t scan my gift card so I wanted to make her line longer so she will be more busy ,so I decided to explain everything in Chinese for her but she still didn’t understand I tried again to explain to her but she said that she don’t need to scan both gift card to activate it when I showed her the gift card says activate at the cashier so I just gave up and I just took the card and left the shop because the $30 is my savings for one month

I suggest that they should try to learn some English
Fresh and quality poultry! They now have pineapple-fed chickens too and is even tastier than normal chickens! Price also very reasonable, advance orders comes with vacuum pack services somemore!
The chicken is so fresh and the owners and staff are friendly. Plus, the price is great! And the delivery of fresh groceries is super convenient.
Really impressed with the freshness and quality of the chicken here. The staff were friendly and helpful, and the grocery delivery was so convenient. Definitely a store I’ll keep using.
Responsive seller! Delivery was fast and efficient! The crabs were fresh and value for money! Will definitely patronise them again!

Please go elsewhere if u want what u ordered.

I placed an order for red grouper days in advance, but on the scheduled delivery day, I was informed there was no stock available. I was not notified of any potential issues with my order beforehand. This is completely unreliable.

The shop owner initiated the cancellation before offering any alternatives. If you dismiss every customer's question about why this wasn't communicated prior to placing the order as abusive, then that's your own perspective.  Unfortunately, this is a he-said, she-said situation.

And to correct, 90% of my order value was for 2 red groupers that was meant to host my guests for the same day. Unsure how the owner deemed to be just one item that was missing from the whole order. Also,  the shop owner states that "the whole wet market did not have grouper this morning", not sure why, but i managed to get my red grouper from the wet market on the same morning after my order was cancelled.

I wish you all the best in your business. :)

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
Tried some of their food during boutique fair 2025. It was ok, but it’s really nothing special considering the price. The har gao and siew Mai are quite mushy. Kong Bak Pao isn’t fantastic either, just normal. Noodles and dumpling are alright.

Tried their food again at their original location and still not impressed with it especially the Har Gao. Skin is too thick and meat is not substantial. The rest are also mediocre.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
Bought the festive premium pork feasting platter for christmas and the only thing that is premium is the word premium printed on the label. I followed the instructions on the packaging for preparation. Instructions were easy to follow, but that was the only good thing in this meal.

The ribs were extremely tough - worse than the ones you get at cold storage. Under seasoned and had to add my own bbq sauce to make it somewhat edible. Ate 2 ribs and threw the rest away.

The porchetta is fatty and bland. Ate one mouthful and threw the rest away.

Sausages were too salty.

Meatballs were ok, but you can probably get the same quality from the super market.

For $82, you’re better off spending your money elsewhere. I dare say Mcdonald’s and Saizeriya tastes better than this.

This might be the worst Christmas meal I’ve ever had in my life. Perhaps maybe even one of the worst meals I’ve ever had in my life.

The company should stop selling these sets immediately.

Would have given -100 stars on google reviews if google allowed me to, but I guess I’ll have to settle for 1 star which in my opinion, is too generous.

Thanks for ruining my Christmas. You guys should rename your company to “The Grinch”.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text: Ah Ma’s handmade Yong Tau Foo it’s really bad. I couldn’t even go for second bite. The fish paste inside was really hard as well.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
First time online ordering from them. Usually I went to the physical store.

Have to compliment them on how well the items were packed. The green were in a recycle bag And there’s ice pack in between each layers of frozen items.

My daughter loves their Siew Mai and I love the garlic chilli. 🥰 Oh! The pork ribs are really fresh too. Used it to cook bbq ribs and it’s really yummy.
Trying some new items this round. Looking forward to more purchase with them.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text: The Signature Pineapple Tart tin is not sealed. Inside it is a ziplock bag (also with no safety seal) containing the tarts. Quality of the tarts is below expectation too, since the small tin is priced at almost $28. Feels like this shop had bought some cheap tarts and repacked it for resale.

Amazing staff here, they helped with all my queries and application to the gym!

Equipments are new and great. Would highly recommend this gym for your next workout!

The newly opened gym at Parc Point in Tengah. The facility looks modern and well equipped, with a good range of cardio machines, strength equipment, and free weights.
The space is clean and well organised, making it comfortable for both beginners and regular gym-goers.
Like other Anytime Fitness locations, members can access the gym 24/7, which is convenient for different schedules.
A good addition to the neighbourhood for residents looking for a convenient place to work out. 💪
4 squat racks, nice vibe, helpful, friendly and attentive staff!
first time here with a friend and we loved it! super clean, great vibes, and a solid range of machines. the staff were really friendly too! appreciate the warm welcome! 😊

Had a very positive experience with Bella at Anytime Fitness. She was friendly, welcoming, and professional throughout the entire session. Bella gave us a detailed tour of the gym, clearly explained the different membership plans, and shared the benefits of joining AF.

This is the main building of University Sports Centre, also known as USC. Located opposite Yusof Ishak House, this three-storey facility boasts features like swimming pools, squash courts, a gymnasium, and a rock-climbing wall. It’s also effectively a sheltered path for those travelling to Valour House from the direction of YIH.
Home of Team NUS. There are plenty of sports facilities here including swimming pool and squash courts.

The sports centre houses a variety of sports facilities, including two olympic-sized swimming pools (indoor and outdoor), gym, squash, among others.

The swimming pool is generally clean, and may get busy at times. I also particularly appreciate the clean changing rooms/toilet.
Well managed sports centre with vibrant community.
Sports complex for basketball, swimming, squash, table tennis etc. for NUS students and staff.

Golden Village is a reliable cinema option in the west, located conveniently within Jurong Point near Boon Lay MRT. The cineplex has multiple screening halls with comfortable seating and good screen and sound quality, making it suitable for regular movie outings.

Ticketing is efficient with self-service kiosks and automated entry gates, which helps reduce waiting time during peak hours. The cinema typically screens a wide range of movies, from mainstream releases to popular blockbusters.

The vibe is casual and family-friendly, though it can get bustling during weekends and evening shows. Being inside Jurong Point also means easy access to food outlets and shops before or after your movie.

A practical choice if you’re looking for a straightforward and comfortable cinema experience in the Jurong area. 🎬🍿

Watched hoppers last night, last showtime of the day. Ordered a mix of sweet and salted popcorn, but some of the popcorn tasted weird, like antiseptic. It did have a numbing sensation. And it was mostly from the left side of my popcorn box (not sure if this even matters). Thankfully we already read the other review before we visited, so we stopped ourselves from consuming the rest of the popcorn 💔💔 Never had such issues with other GV outlets… please QC!!

24 Nov 2025, Went for Jurong GV, 11am Wicked for Good. Delay for 39 mins, picture of not align, seeing blur movie, later got picture no music, later got movie no picture. Could not finish movie because of delay. What GV is trying to do? Look more like a scam... Need to report to CASE for such issue. It so irresponsible. Spoil the whole program for the family days. Some audience left.

At 12pm, the staff ask the rest of the audience whether they would like to move to another theater to see the movie properly.

Haven’t been to the cinema in forever and finally went to catch Zootopia 2 but the seats smells here (Theatre 4). Wonder if it’s because not much people watch movies these days so the hygiene level dropped?

3 stars cause seat is pretty comfy and there’s Ijooz now which you can bring into the theatre! It’s priced $1 higher than other machine tho. GV toilets v big and clean.

It was very disappointing this time. I have come to golden village a lot of times before but this has not happened before. There was this staff who shouted at me when I was buying the popcorn. We said sweet for the popcorn but ended up scooping salty. Hence we had to stop her and tell her it was sweet. However she had already done it so we had to get mixed or salty. And she shouted at us whether sweet or salty in a very disrespectful manner. In the end we had to get mixed. It was a very a disappointing experience.

It is a vibrant hub offering food like Burger King, a gym, basketball and table tennis courts, and more. It hosts events such as career fairs and volunteer opportunities. Conveniently located near West Coast Park, Clementi Woods Park, Kent Ridge Secondary School, West Coast Market, and West Coast Plaza, it serves as an active and accessible spot for residents to eat, exercise, and participate in community activities.
There is a lion dance troupe that practices every Friday at 7

Exchanged my steps tracker there by appointment. Had my trace together token exchanged due to flat battery too. A convenient and airy space. There is a fast food outlet there. There are ATM machines around too. A good location to book private hired cars too.

Review: September 2022
Staff at the CC are polite & helpful & hope that it remains such.  The  management should regulate training offen & remind their role in community center .   The facilities are getting better and useful for all.
The POSB VTM alongside the ATM'S.
Video teller machine (VTM) is an electronic banking outlet that allows customers to complete basic transactions, request banking services or receive immediate assistance with the help of live video conferencing consultation with a remote bank teller.

Review : November 2021
The staff working aren't trained well in their interacting skills , more offen than not during my visits which I've noticed.
Just as I was registering my entry,  distance away from those seated staff & they questioned me as to what I wanted .
The committee club is obviously for everyone who needs to be updated or for a particular cause.
The staff should be well mannered & ethical to handle any visitor , by NOT being prejudice.

Great place for community activities, event hostings. There is a big hall, basket ball court. Do check out for the upcoming events on the notice board. Extra curricular activities, dance, music classes are also held for children. Had a chance to view the FIFA World Cup 2022 final screening.

place_name: Dig Dig Land | Kid Excavator & Playground Singapore
review_text:
Fantastic space for kids, especially those fascinated by powerful machines like cranes and excavators. The park offers several arenas for exploration, including a sandpit, a real crane/excavator site, an area for hands-on tool exploration and many others!

Do note that it’s an outdoor venue with minimal shelter, so rainy days may affect the experience, and it can get hot depending on the weather.

Overall, the kids had a wonderful time and it’s an engaging and fun environment for young little engineers in the making.

Toilets available though located at the building at the side and parking though limited is also available right outside.

place_name: Dig Dig Land | Kid Excavator & Playground Singapore
review_text: It’s very well maintained and even though it was hot there are some awnings for shade. Excavators are very realistic and it was interesting even for the adults! It’s a small area but the kids could entertain themselves for one hour with no issue.

place_name: Dig Dig Land | Kid Excavator & Playground Singapore
review_text: We had such a wonderful time here today. The kids loved playing with all the toys and dad enjoyed driving the excavator as well. Plenty of parking and showers free to use afterwards to rinse off and cool down. Highly recommended 👌

place_name: Dig Dig Land | Kid Excavator & Playground Singapore
review_text:
Fun concept, but price feels too high

DigDig Land has a really nice concept — plenty of space, very few crowds, and the kids definitely had fun. The place is well maintained and feels safe, and the excavators and cranes are the highlight of the visit.

However, the price is honestly exaggerated for what you get: $38 per kid + $10 per adult for only 1 hour. Considering it’s an outdoor attraction, only 3 excavators and 2 cranes are powered activities. The rest is basically a sandpit with toys, which makes the cost feel hard to justify. If it were indoors, the pricing would make more sense, but for an outdoor setup it feels quite expensive compared to the facility provided.

Overall, it’s a good activity and the kids enjoyed themselves — just be prepared for the higher price tag.

place_name: Dig Dig Land | Kid Excavator & Playground Singapore
review_text: Every kid's dream activity! A lovely little outdoor play park with sand and diggers big and small! Very hot on a steamy day, but they have showers where you can cool down and wash off the sand afterwards. Each slot is 1hr and you can pre-book online. Highly recommended as a special treat. Great for 2 - 12 year olds.
Playground in jungle but really nice done. For small and big ones. Space to rest, lots of shade but sad they closed a caffe on the top of the hill.
It was an OK playground for kids below 12 years old. It consisted of some local themes such as a giant Chempedak and saga seeds. Would be great if there is at least one F&B nearby.

COMO Adventure Grove is not just a place for kids’ activities and fun, but also a wonderful spot to enjoy nature. 🌳 For us, it turned out to be a great area for jogging and walking and refreshing, spacious, and relaxing. It’s a nice balance of play, exercise, and nature all in one place. Definitely worth a visit whether you’re here for activities or just to stay active. 🚶🏻‍♂️🏃🏻‍♂️✨

An interesting playground for older kids. A sand pit with a very large durian-shaped structure and a Saga seed themed sand filled play area. There are swings and interesting slides. But the downside is, it can get rather hot on sunny day.

An adventure playground area located within the beautiful Botanic Garden and specifically in the Gallop Extension section. Kids would love the options like sand pits, swings and slides which are quite extensive. While the walking path is sometime shady, this part of the playground is in the open so plenty of sun.

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026

Zen vibes in this garden. Don't remember it being done up quite like this when I'd visited in school. They've done a good job here.

Don't need to go to Bali to find that trademark Indonesian half gate. Am interested in finding out more about this type of architecture, and why it's done this way, perhaps there is some religious significance?

Someone chopped off the heads of about 1/2 the doves!

The way the pools mute ambient sound is very soothing as well.

Nestled within the lush greenery of Fort Canning Park, the Sang Nila Utama Garden is a tranquil retreat perfect for relaxation. This beautifully designed garden draws inspiration from the 14th-century palaces of Southeast Asian rulers, offering a serene and rejuvenating environment for visitors to unwind.

The garden features elegant landscaping, intricate Javanese split gates, and calming water features that create a peaceful ambiance. Surrounded by greenery, it feels like stepping into a different era, far removed from the hustle and bustle of the city.

Whether you're looking for a quiet spot to reflect, a place to connect with nature, or simply a relaxing break during your walk in Fort Canning Park, the Sang Nila Utama Garden is an ideal choice. Its calming atmosphere makes it a great spot to recharge your mind and body.

With its historical significance and serene beauty, this garden is a hidden gem that combines culture, nature, and peace in one perfect location. It’s truly a rejuvenating escape worth exploring for anyone visiting Fort Canning Park.

🌿Sang Nila Utama Garden at Fort Canning Park takes on a Javanese style landscape.
⛲The Garden features symmetrical layout, reflective ponds and Javanese style split gates that marks the entrance into a peaceful and tranquil space.
🪻🥀The entire Sang Nila Utama Garden is landscaped with beautiful fragrant ornamental flowering plants that emits a subtle fragrance as you walk pass them.
🪔Sang Nila Utama was an ancient king of Singapore.

Hidden within Fort Canning Park, the Sang Nila Utama Garden is a truly peaceful and tranquil hidden gem. Not many tourists here, making it ideal for a quiet appreciation of nature and history.

This meticulously recreated 14th-century Royal Garden honors Singapore's founder, Sang Nila Utama, marking the site of the first king's palace. You'll notice distinctive Javanese split gates and symmetrical layouts, reminiscent of ancient Balinese temples. Charming statues of frogs, fish, and ducks add to the authentic feel.

The garden's unique architecture and lush greenery make it incredibly "Instagram-worthy." Its unparalleled serenity is a major draw; during my visit, it was blissfully quiet.

If you're in the Fort Canning area, definitely stop by the Sang Nila Utama Garden. It's a refreshing retreat and a beautiful step back in time.
Is a remarkable ancient architecture and structure who show how ancient kingdom once stood on this land. Although it is just part of the garden but can already feel the ancient harmony atmosphere.

Gardens by the Bay is truly an amazing place and definitely a must-visit attraction in Singapore. The entire area is beautifully designed, extremely clean, and very well maintained, making it a wonderful experience for visitors of all ages.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest offer a unique opportunity to see plants and flowers from different parts of the world. During our visit, we also experienced the Tulip Feast, where we saw many beautiful varieties of tulips in full bloom. The colorful display was absolutely stunning and added a vibrant touch to the overall experience. The indoor waterfall inside the Cloud Forest is especially impressive and creates a refreshing atmosphere. The Supertree Grove is another major highlight, and the night light show there feels absolutely magical with the music and colorful lights.

The whole place is spacious and easy to explore, although there is a lot to walk around, so it can feel a little tiring after some time. Still, the beautiful views and attractions make the walking completely worth it.

There are many perfect photo spots throughout the gardens, especially during the evening when the night view looks stunning. Souvenir shops are also available if you would like to buy gifts or memories from your visit.

Overall, it’s a peaceful and beautiful place to relax, enjoy nature, and experience one of Singapore’s most iconic attractions. Highly recommended, especially for first-time visitors.

Gardens by the Bay is truly a highlight of Singapore and absolutely not to be missed. I’d highly recommend getting a combo ticket for the Cloud Forest and Flower Dome—plan for about 2-3 hours to cover both.

The Cloud Forest is spectacular, almost Jurassic Park-like in parts, and enjoyable for both kids and adults. There’s misting at specific times, so it’s worth checking those in advance. A great way to explore is to head to the top level first and then make your way down, stopping at the different exhibits along the way.
The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, showcasing flowers and plants from around the world, with plenty of photo-worthy corners throughout. There’s also a nice gift shop with garden and flower-themed souvenirs.

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

Gardens by the Bay is an amazing place to visit in Singapore. The gardens are beautifully maintained, with lots of greenery, walking paths, water features and great photo spots everywhere you look.

Even on a cloudy day after some rain, the place still looked beautiful and peaceful. The views of Marina Bay Sands and the Supertrees in the background are incredible, and there’s plenty to see just walking around the outdoor areas.

It’s clean, well organised and easy to explore. Some attractions cost extra, but the free garden areas alone are worth visiting. Definitely one of the best places to see in Singapore.

A beautiful beach on Sentosa. Some of the venues were closed for us being the off tourist season but that does not take from the beautiful scenery and the great beach shuttle bus that is free along the beach.

Palawan Beach_Sentosa
City lives can be stressed 😣. So sometimes we need to take a break 🫷. Pull ourselves out of the loop 🔁. Lay back 🏖, relax and enjoy the stunning seaview 🏝. Feel the sea breeze 🌊 blowing on us while sipping a cup of cool drinks 🍹 and listen to our favourite music 🎶🎵.
