Sentosa is a popular resort island of Singapore. A major tourist and recreation hub.
​Its name, meaning "peace and tranquility" in Malay. The island has many exciting attractions, which include:
*Various restaurants, cafes, bars, fast foods, etc.
*​Universal Studios Singapore.
*Singapore Oceanarium.
*Various retail shops.
*The Weave at Resorts World Sentosa.
*​Adventure Cove Waterpark.
*​Three beaches (Siloso, Palawan, and Tanjong).
*​A variety of historical sites, resorts.
*Adventure activities like the Skyline Luge and iFly Singapore.

​It's easily accessible from the mainland via a bridge, cable car, monorail, and pedestrian boardwalk.

Very nice beach and worth the trip to Sentosa island! Go to southern-most point of central Asia, got that of my bucket list. Really nice beach and a good place to swim (if no crocs around)
Outlook towers are awesome, you can get a great 360° look
Very clean- as per Singaporean standard! Will come back again!
This beach is gorgeous! 🌊✨ Lovely sands and amazing vibes. Had a fantastic time!

The beach was so beautiful, clean, it will make you very comfortable just staying there to enjoy the scene. However the weather can get to hot and humid. The sea view probably not so special as it was filled with plenty of ships

It is a lovely and relaxing spot, located close to Palawan Beach and easily accessible from Beach Station. There are convenient amenities nearby, including a 7-Eleven, making it easy to grab drinks or snacks. The beach has a laid-back atmosphere, and it’s common to see people playing volleyball, frisbee, or simply enjoying the sand. It’s also a great place for a picnic or to unwind while watching the sunset. Overall, it’s a convenient and enjoyable beach for both leisure and casual activities.

We went just to see it by our own eyes. It was not something you will be amazed if. It was okay. The sand was imported from Australia. The activities looks okay. I think most local would love it but as foreigner, it was just nice to look and enjoy the view. A nearby restaurant available. Walking distance from most food stalls.

Very clean, handy free transport link options between beaches. Lots of activities and eateries.  Easy access for those with poor mobility, pushchairs, etc
Family friendly
Beautiful beach! There is a free shuttle bus around siloso beach.
A beautiful, clean, and safe beach. Filled with tourists and Singaporean locals alike. I once spent a quiet holiday here with someone I loved.

Kallang Park Connector is an absolute gem for anyone who appreciates a bit of wilderness in the heart of the city. As a regular spot for jogging, it offers much more than just a paved path; the air is incredibly fresh, making those morning or evening runs feel revitalizing. What really sets this route apart, though, is the incredible biodiversity. During my visits, I’ve encountered everything from vibrant insects to the occasional snakes in the river—nature is definitely thriving here.

For those who find themselves constantly looking toward the water, the sightings of various fish species are a fantastic bonus that adds to the charm of the area. It’s the perfect blend of a functional fitness route and a mini-safari. Whether you're working on your cardio or just looking to clear your head, the connection to nature here is top-notch. If you're looking for an all-in-one spot to stay active and enjoy the outdoors, this is the place to be. Highly recommended for nature enthusiasts and fitness lovers alike!

Kallang Park Connector is a linear green trail in Singapore’s Park Connector Network (PCN) that runs along the Kallang River and links Bishan-Ang Mo Kio Park in the north to Kallang Riverside Park in the south.

It’s about 7.87 km long and provides a scenic route for walking, jogging, and cycling through several central residential areas

We definitely enjoyed our walk along the Kallang PCN.

There's a weird sign pointing towards bishan park (the wrong way), confused us for a moment.

One of the most scenic park connector in Singapore linking Clark Quay and City center to Kallang, Kallang Bahru, Potong Pasir and all the way to Bishan Park. Perfect for cycling, jogging and brisk walking.
Very good area for relax your mind

The 2 stretches of Kallang Park connector that go under the bridges are finally open this week! Lovely artwork. Kudos to the artists that put them up and pat on the back for the workers whp were involved in the construction.  Joggers and cyclists can now bypass 2 traffic lights at Kallang Bahru and Upper Boon Keng Road, to get from Bendemeer Road to Kallang MRT. Celebration!
It’s amazing how nature has the power to uplift the soul and inspire hope, especially when one comes across an unexpected abundance of blooms.
Park connector linking from Ubi/Paya Lebar/ Macpherson. It leads you to Lor Ah Soo and nearby commercial offices too.

review_text: Our child transferred from China to K2 at Raffles Kidz Pres. Thanks to all teachers’ patient care, especially Chinese teacher Wen, he adapted fast and enjoys school life now. We are satisfied with the school’s warm environment and teaching.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
Our son has been in school for 1.5yrs since he was 18mo. During transition from PN to Nursery was when we were given feedback on his development, and at which point we considered changing schools for something that was more suitable for him. Principal Gwen stepped in and offered a change in class and teachers and we agreed to see if this allowed our son to enjoy school and learn better. We are so glad we were encouraged to this step because fast forward 6m later, our son has shown so much growth in social, emotional, physical & cognitive development. We must especially commend Teacher Arinah and Xing Lao Shi, his form teachers, to whom both have been very dedicated in caring and guiding our son along the way. Particularly, there was more feedback provided to us either by phone calls or after school drop off chats — that helped us to also support the teachers work in helping our son’s progress. Our son has since been much happier in going and talking about school.

We would also like to thank Teacher Adillah, Zann and Anna who also made school so much more enjoyable with their warmth and investment to really engaging (chatting) with our boy!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text: The teachers are always so welcoming during drop-off and pick-up which gives parents great peace of mind. We transferred our son here and it has definitely been the right choice. The teachers have been patient with him especially during meal times and we’ve seen great progress that he is now toilet trained. Lessons are also well rounded and engaging. place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
Raffles Kidz has been an amazing preschool experience for our daughter. From the very first day, the teachers made her feel incredibly warm, welcomed, and safe. You can really feel their dedication — they take the time to understand each child’s personality, and they create an environment where the kids are excited to learn and explore.
Our daughter looks forward to school every morning, and we’ve seen her grow so much in confidence, language, and social skills. The curriculum is engaging, the activities are thoughtfully planned, and the entire team communicates well with parents.
We’re truly grateful to the teachers and staff at Raffles Kidz for giving our little one such a happy and nurturing start to her learning journey. Highly recommended for any parent looking for a caring and enriching preschool!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
My kids have attended Raffles Kidz since Pre-Nursery and I can say with confidence, “No regrets at all!”

The school compound is spacious, neat, safe and definitely a fun learning environment for the kids to be in. Special mention to Tr Triff, 牛老师, Tr Rosz and 张老师!

Tr Triff and Niu Laoshi have played a significant role in my boy’s early education journey, building a strong foundation for my son as he graduates from K2 this year. place_name: Kokoro Izakaya (FairPrice Hub - Joo Koon MRT)
review_text:
Donkey, Aerin, and I visited Kokoro Izakaya based on a TikTok recommendation praising its vibrant atmosphere and quality food. The restaurant has warm, cosy decor with a dedicated band area, which likely livens up in the evenings with live music. However, during our afternoon visit, the place was very quiet, with few patrons and staff mostly sitting idle.

We arrived with high expectations but found the lunch menu quite limited, with many desirable dishes available only at dinner. We wanted to try the beef cubes, so we asked if they could prepare them outside the regular menu. The staff kindly agreed. When the beef cubes arrived, the portion was small for the price, and the beef was overcooked beyond our preferred medium rare and not top grade.

We also ordered the seafood ramen, which was decent but lacked excitement, and the California Maki, which was presented untidily with unevenly distributed fish roe and an odd taste. The Chawanmushi was watery and lacked proper egg consistency. The sashimi portion was acceptable but the wasabi lacked a real kick. The Beef Don was cooked perfectly and was the highlight of the meal.

The yuzu mochi was refreshing and sweet, making for a pleasant end to the meal. We initially planned to order garlic fried rice but decided against it after trying the other dishes. Donkey ordered a Highball, but the ice was regular cubes instead of spherical, and the alcohol-to-mix balance was off, making the drink less enjoyable. We noticed the restaurant remained largely empty during dinner hours, with staff sitting around idly, which was disappointing.

Despite the nice decor and potential lively atmosphere, the overall experience did not meet expectations due to small portions, inconsistent food quality, and quiet ambiance. Aerin also mentioned the food was not particularly enjoyable. Considering all factors, I’d rate Kokoro Izakaya 3 stars—a cosy spot with promise but lacking vibrancy and consistent quality during our visit.

Ratings:

- Food Quality: 3/5 – Some decent dishes but inconsistent overall
- Service: 3/5 – Staff were polite but somewhat inattentive during quiet periods
- Ambiance: 3/5 – Nice decor but lacked the lively atmosphere expected
- Value for Money: 2.5/5 – Portions felt small relative to price
- Overall Experience: 3/5 – A cosy spot with potential but needs improvement in food and vibe

place_name: Kokoro Izakaya (FairPrice Hub - Joo Koon MRT)
review_text: The food was delicious and nicely presented. I really enjoyed the ambience of the restaurant, it felt cosy and welcoming. I was pleasantly surprised to find that there was live music too, and the singer sang very well, which added to the whole dining experience. The waitress who served us was also very friendly and polite. Overall, a great place to dine and relax. I will definitely recommend this place to my friends and family!

place_name: Kokoro Izakaya (FairPrice Hub - Joo Koon MRT)
review_text: Delicious meal, we ordered wagyu don and Ramen. Service was top notch, they attend to us immediately when needed. Their refillable tea was delicious. They have live band every Thursday and Friday which we missed because we came on Monday. So we will come back again for sure! 🥳

place_name: Kokoro Izakaya (FairPrice Hub - Joo Koon MRT)
review_text: We had a fantastic dinner here. The Japanese food is excellent, and the atmosphere is very cozy and welcoming. The staff provided great service, making our evening very enjoyable. I will definitely be returning, and I highly recommend it to others.

place_name: Kokoro Izakaya (FairPrice Hub - Joo Koon MRT)
review_text:
Kitty, Aerin, and I visited Kokoro Izakaya based on a TikTok recommendation, which praised its vibrant atmosphere and highly rated food. The restaurant features a warm, cosy decor and has a dedicated band area, which I believe makes it lively during the evenings when live music is performed. During our visit in the afternoon, however, the place was very quiet, with only a few patrons and the staff sitting idly.

We arrived with high expectations but soon realised the lunch menu was quite limited, with many of the dishes we wanted only available at dinner. Kitty and Aerin were keen to try the beef cubes, so we asked the staff if they could prepare them outside the usual menu. They kindly obliged. However, when the beef cubes arrived, we were surprised by the small portion relative to the price. The beef was not of top grade and, unfortunately, was overcooked beyond our preferred medium rare.

We also ordered the seafood ramen, which was decent but lacked the ‘wow’ factor, and the California Maki, which was presented somewhat untidily with unevenly distributed fish roe and a strange taste that was hard to identify. The Chawanmushi was watery, more like a soup, with the egg insufficiently cooked. The sashimi portion was acceptable, but the wasabi failed to deliver any real kick. The Beef Don was cooked perfectly and was the most satisfying dish of the meal.

The yuzu mochi was quite nice, refreshing and sweet, providing a lovely ending to our meal. We initially wanted to order the garlic fried rice but decided against it after sampling the other dishes. I also ordered a Highball, but the ice was not the typical spherical shape and consisted of regular cubes. The drink’s alcohol-to-mix ratio felt imbalanced, making it less enjoyable. After our meal, we noticed the restaurant remained largely unoccupied, even during dinner hours, with staff sitting around idle, which was quite disappointing.

Although the decor was nice and the atmosphere appeared promising, the overall experience did not meet our expectations due to the small portions, inconsistent food quality, and the quiet ambience during dinner. Aerin mentioned that the food wasn’t particularly enjoyable either. Considering all factors, I would rate this place 3 stars, a cosy spot with potential but lacking in vibrancy and food consistency during our visit.

review_author_name: সিংগাপুর টু ঢাকা SINGAPORE TO DHAKA
review_text: good

Went to the outlet (Tagore) to place an order for CNY, to make sure that delivery would be in place for reunion dinner.

Food was delivered just before the scheduled delivery time, which was exactly what we needed. Quality was good and tasted fresh. The fishballs and beef balls were especially well received.

Will use them again next time we do a steamboat.

What a great wet market, combined with Sheng Siong, lots of independent small stores, food court. The prices are fantastic compare to NTUC Fair price at the wet market. There’s heap of hawker food too, especially busy were prata, chicken rice, Lor mee and bee hoon and curry chicken. Nice atmosphere in the weekend and old nostalgia where your ah-Ma would love to shop here. Just wished it had more meat and vegetable stores in the wet market section. Good place to show the kids and spend the morning in the weekend.

I highly recommend this butcher!  Prime Pork ribs are very high quality, smooth, tender and fresh.  Their minced pork is the perfect meat/ fat ratio, very fresh.  No rancid smell at all.  I’ve also purchased their deboned chicken (quarter) 500gm pack many times- used it for grilling, steaming, braising.  Also recommend their pork meat balls- very tasty, has cuttlefish in it.  They deliver the items in an insulated box, full of ice packs to keep the meat cold and safe.  Everything is vacuum packed.  I’m so pleased with their service.  Their pricing is also competitive.  The thing with going to the wet market physically, you still need to repack into your desired portions upon coming home, so ordering from quan shui really saves me a lot of time and energy.

I always love going to the wet markets but cannot afford the time to wake up super early to get the freshest produce! Love that they deliver to your doorstep and even give the option to recycle the ice packs/boxes. The selection of produce is great, quality is really good and worth every bit of the slight premium!! Fruits & meat are super fresh! Ready to eat section is great for quick meals too! 100% will recommend!

We cooked with the wild caught balai threadfin for porridge, Silverfern short rib shabu shabu for Korean beef Bulgogi, silverfern ribeye steak for Beef bowl. It was amazing! 👌

place_name: Fullerton Health Medical Centre for Migrant Workers @ Gul Circle
review_text:
Very poor and frustrating experience.
The entire process is extremely slow — appointment queue, doctor consultation queue, and especially the dispensary. If a person is already sick and comes here, there is no chance to take proper rest. I arrived at 2:15 PM and it is already 5:00 PM, still stuck waiting.
The dispensary is painfully slow, with no sense of urgency. I am writing this feedback with a headache while sitting in the waiting queue, which itself explains how bad the system is. Patients are not treated like patients here, but like numbers. This level of delay is unacceptable for a clinic.

review_text:
Poor service has been provided by the health professional. Staff can be seen yelling at migrant workers. The health professional assigned to conduct blood tests was using his phone to watch shows while performing the task.

This is one of the official channels appointed by MOM for all migrants in the construction sector to visit regarding health and medical issues.

I urge MOM to review and conduct mystery spot checks on them. It is a shame that Singapore is treating these workers with such inadequate medical facilities. I will write to MOM and provide feedback on this matter as well.

Sundial Garden is one of the quieter and more elegant corners of the Singapore Botanic Gardens. The combination of symmetrical brick pathways, reflective ponds, sculptures, and manicured greenery gives the place a very peaceful and almost European garden feel

The sundial itself is beautifully positioned at the centre, and the surrounding water features and plants make it a relaxing spot to slow down and enjoy the scenery. I also liked the decorative stone urns and carefully maintained landscaping throughout the area

Most people stick to the main paths, but it’s worth wandering a bit further to find this spot since it feels completely different from the rest of the park. It’s a formal, classical-style garden that’s very symmetrical and peaceful.

The highlight is the old sundial in the middle, which has been there since the 1920s. If you look closely at the base, there are some really cool, slightly eerie carvings and a deep inscription that makes you stop and think for a second. The four little lily ponds at the corners and the manicured hedges give it a very elegant, "old-school" feel.

It’s a very quiet here, which kinda feels like this is deserted place within the Singapore Botanic Gardens. It’s just a simple, refined area to take a break and enjoy the greenery in a more structured setting.

The Sundial Garden is quite hidden and you have to climb some staircases to reach there. There are nice four sided ponds with a statue on each corner.

Unfortunately, there was a woman doing a photoshot there, so I couldn't take a panoramic video shot in one go.

The pools smell stagnant though, and it's obvious. I don’t see the water moving so maybe that is the case. Fortunately, there were no mosquitoes.

I was there in the morning on quite a cloudly day, so unfortunately, I didn’t see how the sundial work.

A small garden and the only one within the Botanic Gardens that's designed in formal, classical style. The rest of the gardens follow the English landscape style. It's laid out in a rectangular style, forming a cross in the middle and creating four small lilly ponds.
The Sundial Garden in Singapore Botanic Gardens is a peaceful spot with a classic sundial centerpiece surrounded by lush greenery. A quiet place to pause and take in the garden’s charm.

So many collections of different books and so many different levels to go and explore! They have a community space for events and workshops , then there is a cafe and bakery inside with good food options. There is so many different things to explore! I couldn’t even finish going through all the sections! I will definitely go there again soon. The place is also located very close to the MRT  station and the Jurong east bus terminus . They will announce over the mike before the closing time so you have time to pack your stuff. I especially liked the photography section and farming section . There’s a separate magazine section and newspaper section as well. Different languages from Tamil to Malay to Korean you will find in the library .

This library was visited few times in the span of 15 years. Still impressed by its cleanliness, calm and  peaceful atmosphere in addition to the silent air, the display of new arrivals also catch my eyes. Always full of study-loaded students and other book-lovers, you will still find a seat for enjoying some books or surfing internet on the provided PC's as the library is very spacious. Remarkably, there is a vast collection of books.

Joo Koon MRT Station is an elevated MRT station on the East–West Line in western Singapore. It mainly serves the Joo Koon industrial area and is coded EW29. Location and basic infoThe station is located at 91 Joo Koon Circle, Singapore 629116, near the junction of Joo Koon Circle, Benoi Road and International Road.  It sits beside FairPrice Hub and functions as a key access point for nearby factories and logistics facilities. Line and operationsJoo Koon is on the green East–West Line between Pioneer (towards Pasir Ris/Changi Airport) and Gul Circle/Tuas Link (towards Tuas).  It has an elevated island platform operated by SMRT Trains, with trains running both towards central Singapore and towards Tuas. Exits and nearby placesThere are two main exits:Exit A towards Joo Koon Bus Interchange, FairPrice Hub and Benoi Road. Exit B towards Upper Jurong Road, Arena Country Club, Singapore Discovery Centre and nearby SAF camps. Bus connectionsRight next to the station is Joo Koon Bus Interchange, from which many trunk and industrial services operate into Jurong and Tuas.  Additional bus stops along Benoi Road and Joo Koon Circle are served by routes such as 192, 193, 252, 253, 254, 255, 257, 258 and 258M.

Joo Koon MRT Station (EW29) is an elevated station located along Singapore’s East-West Line, serving the Joo Koon district within the larger Jurong Industrial Estate. Opened in 2011, it was built to support the heavy commuter flow generated by the numerous factories, logistics hubs, and industrial facilities in the surrounding area. As the former western terminus of the East-West Line before the opening of Tuas Link, the station played a key role in improving accessibility for workers traveling daily to the western region.

The station features a simple, functional design with an island platform layout, sheltered walkways, and direct connectivity to the nearby bus interchange. This makes it a key transfer point for commuters heading to various industrial zones, including those located deeper within Tuas. Joo Koon MRT Station is also situated near amenities such as Joo Koon Circle, FairPrice Hub, and assorted eateries that cater to the working population.

Beyond its transport function, the station is part of broader efforts to enhance connectivity to Singapore’s western growth areas. With ongoing development in Jurong and Tuas—such as the Tuas Mega Port and the Jurong Innovation District—the station continues to serve as an important gateway for the region’s evolving economic landscape.

This is a busy station in the morning. People walk through the Mall and mostly will stop over at the level 1 bakery shop to load up carbs. The KopiTiam serves hearty breakfast set at $2.20 for NTUC union members. Coffee is quite nicely brewed.

It seems that most of the time the train will end in this Joo Koon station instead of the Tuas Link station. This is a very remote area in the extreme west part of Singapore. It is nice to know that they have a FairPrice Hub mall that is linked to the train station that offers quite a lot of F&B outlets. Overall, it's a nice mrt station except that there are very few train services that goes to Tuas Link.

The quality of the food can be better. My laksa tastes sweet and my toast one side is burnt, the other side is barely toasted.

Ordered the green curry beef tendon with Thai noodles and a hot milk tea. Milk tea was ok but the noodles were lumpy and difficult to chew, and the curry was overly sweet. The tendons were overdone and mushy. Won't visit again.
Coffee pistachio is a new experience for me. Small store but have three table for dine in. People here are chill. Coffee were served in less then 5 mins

They should not allow non paying people occupy the seats & tables that have been allocated for the paying ones. Otherwise service levels is above average. Another inconsiderate person. Occupying the seat and watching video on her mobile even though she didn’t get anything

Ordered from this outlet.

Biggest disappointment is when there is no straw even for frappe. Had to wait for the frappe to melt to drink and it taste horrible.
Not sure if they dont provide any or they simply forgot to pack.

The coconut latte was as good as the one I had in Beijing. Rich, thick and yummy. Definitely better than the similar drink I had in other luckin coffee outlet in Singapore. Good atmosphere at evening when the working crowd left for home.

I observed the front-line staff and Mr. Nathan himself providing top-tier customer service throughout the meal. Their commitment to ensuring a great dining experience is rare and much appreciated. Ordered lunch. For $15 u gave only 3 pc of chunk chicken? Total ordered is $19++ for that kind of food potion i think can get better food quantity. Pls be reasonable when doing business. Nowadays yeah all expensive but selling like that kind of quantity. U give who to eat? 🙄🙄

We love it. Their food taste was so great. Mutton is too juicy & delicious. I had try 1st ever soft delicious dosa with their sauce. Lolly pop fried was so crispy, yummy. All those were smell great & worth to eat. Price also fair. So u should try when u  were here. Try delicious India taste lah !! Food were fantastic, I forgot to take photo .

But I'm not sure they have this option in all of their outlets or not.

Update on Tuesday, 5 December 2023 (lunch hour)

Expect to wait a while during lunch hour. We waited 25 mins for our food even tho it's only less than half full. I think the kitchen is short-handed with only 2 chef.. ?

Taste is ok but a bit saltish as before. Serving portion is adequate for the price paid (see photos).

Senior Coach Aaron is truly exceptional. My child is naturally very mild and timid, but under Coach Aaron’s guidance, he blossomed with confidence and skill. In just three months, Coach Aaron’s systematic, step-by-step coaching helped my child master both the butterfly kick and back aerial — something we never imagined possible in such a short time.

His approach is patient, structured, and incredibly effective. Every session was tailored to my child’s pace, yet gently pushed them to grow. The transformation was not just physical, but mental — my child now approaches challenges with far more self-belief.

Highly recommended for 1-on-1 private coaching if you're looking for accelerated progress in a supportive and professional environment. Coach Aaron doesn’t just teach technique — he builds character.

Some pros and cons after my children attended lessons for a while.

First of all, we had to do a review when we first signed up and indicate 5 stars. I indicated 4 stars at first because I felt that I have not start actual classes to be able to give a 5 stars right away, but I was asked by one of the coaches to change to a 5 stars. I personally think that I can give a 5 stars but perhaps after some lessons and if I think my experience is good with them, but perhaps not right at registration. The fact that I was asked to change to 5 stars also may not be the appropriate thing to ask…. Maybe that’s why the reviews are all 5 stars ?
And if you noticed, majority of reviews are about trial classes too…

For beginners, no fixed or assigned coach. It can change every lesson. After a while, it felt more like the children are “training” the coach, because some of the coaches are not as experienced.
And also, there is basically no “continuity/follow up”? The fact that that coaches can change weekly, also mean that your kid’s progress is not monitored closely. This largely applies to the beginners. So perhaps they are also trying to sift out the good students for them to focus on ? 🤷🏻‍♀️
Good if you get coach Aaron or siyuan for all your lessons because they are the more experienced ones.

Lastly, if your kids are still very young, would be good to check on their logistics arrangement if your kid has to go toilet.
I have seen some young kids going toilet on their own. Lucky mine are older and can safely go on their own. They may not have the manpower to arrange for someone to bring the smaller  to toilet at all.

So, all in all, wushu is a good sport for kids to get active and it could also be a CCA for some schools. But with regards to the points highlighted above, perhaps another school may be better ?
Brought my daughter here for her first Wushu class. Coaches are patient and professional, and the environment is very welcoming for beginners. She enjoyed the session and is excited to continue!

Love the teacher-to-student ratio here. Brought my son for his 1st trial class and was really surprise how this experience turns out to be. He had never attended any trials before and he's so attentive and adapted quickly simply because of the attention & guidance by the coaches. Would like to specially give a shoutout to coach SiYuan, who's is very loving and patient with my son being in a new environment. Highly recommended Xuan! Especially the part it's in a supermaket haha can shop while the kids train. Win-win. THUMBS UP
Brought my 2 kids for trial yesterday and they enjoyed themselves alot! Many thanks to Coach Siyuan for his attentive teaching of my 2 kids during the trial lesson.

A small chill place filled with bouncing activities for children. It is located right next to the NTUC Fairprice store in Joo Koon Mall. The outlet is quite long and there are many children playing here when I visited. It's a good place especially on weekends for the children to have some fun and the adults can rest in the chairs provided.
Fun for kids and even has birthday party rooms. Cheap compared to other trampoline parks.
Smaller than the previous one near Jur West canal, but sufficient for an hour's worth of fun with kiddos. Was staffed by young gentlemen who were helpful.
Friendly staff, kid friendly, my girl thoroughly enjoyed herself today. Wonderful trampoline facilities. 🥳
It is late man

Hindhede Nature Park is one of Singapore’s hidden gems for anyone looking to enjoy nature without committing to a long or strenuous hike. Despite its relatively small size, the park packs in an impressive variety of rainforest scenery, peaceful walking trails, and one of the most rewarding viewpoints in the country

The trails are well-maintained and easy to follow, making the park suitable for families, seniors, and casual walkers. Along the way, you’ll find lush tropical vegetation, towering trees, interesting plant life, and plenty of shaded paths that make for a relaxing stroll even on warmer days. The shelters and lookout points are thoughtfully placed, offering great spots to rest and soak in the surroundings

The highlight is undoubtedly the former quarry lookout. The emerald-green water framed by dramatic rock cliffs creates a surprisingly scenic landscape that feels far removed from the city

It seems this is my favourite spot to hike and train myself for my next climb. The trail is like super easy so I do many repetitions. The place is nice. Plenty of wild snakes and monkeys to look for. If you are lucky, juvenile king cobras too.
Nice and peaceful park with an easy walking trail. Great for a short stroll, and the quarry view is really nice.
This is an easy trail and accessible via either from car park or Bukit Timah visitor center. That’s a playground for children age 7-12 also. No admission ticket required.

Such a pretty park ! Super near Bukit Timah Summit so it can be a little outing. Went around noon and I was almost fried by the sun. Lowkey got lost but it was relatively easy to find my way to the Quarry ! Recommend to visit in the late afternoon to avoid the sun hehe. Not sure why they say you need to buy tickets but it’s free !!

As a nearby resident, I visit Jurong Lake Gardens quite frequently. The walking tracks are always well-maintained, and I enjoy the scenic lake views. There are also plenty of shaded paths, which make for a very relaxing walk.

A pleasant but fairly small section of Jurong Lake Gardens. The landscaping is neat and well maintained, and there are some nice plants and boardwalk paths to stroll through, but it doesn’t take very long to cover the entire area

It’s fine to visit if you’re already nearby or doing a full loop of the gardens, but probably not worth going out of your way just for this section alone

The newly renovated Jurong Park @Chinese Garden @Japanese Garden is amazing. Many interesting plants, ponds, and fixtures are unique to only this park. Many themes are nicely decorated.

Food stores are scattered everywhere while facilities like toilets and water dispensers are generously available.

It's a good park for exploration, evening walks, and slow biking.

A huge garden! Took about 2 hours to slowly stroll, enjoy the view and reach the other end of the garden. It’s home to many animals and there’s so much to see. My favorite starts from the Japanese Garden onwards. Really enjoy the peaceful environment there and nice place to totally unwind. It’s a short walking distance from the MRT station.
nice and peaceful place. great for walks, jogs and runs. some do cycle around.

The school itself is excellent, especially the centre head, Miss ZX. She is caring, responsible, and professional, and parents can genuinely feel confident that their children are safe and well looked after under her care. The teachers are also friendly, patient, and motivated, creating a warm and positive environment for the kids.

Unfortunately, the biggest problem is the transport vendor, “Emily Bus,” and parents seriously need to be cautious. Their billing practices feel very unfair and unprofessional. They will pressure parents as early as February, during the CNY period, to pay the remaining bus fees for July to December upfront, claiming it is to “confirm” transport arrangements for the second half of the year. However, this seems like a tactic to lock parents in because once payment is made, they will use it against you and refuse to refund the so-called “deposit” even if you want to cancel before July.

On top of that, there are concerns about overcrowded vans/buses, with children packed tightly inside. The back of the vehicle sometimes has poor or no air-conditioning, which is extremely uncomfortable and honestly raises safety concerns in Singapore’s hot weather. As a parent, it is worrying to imagine kids enduring long rides in these conditions.

The school deserves praise for its leadership and teachers, but the transport service provided by Emily Bus is a serious negative point and urgently needs improvement in terms of transparency, fairness, safety, and professionalism.

I recently visited the center, and I was highly impressed. The environment was not only inviting and cozy but also exceptionally clean.

Ms Junie  was welcoming and very accommodating, making me feel confident about enrolling my child there. What's more, the affordable fees were a pleasant surprise, and the facilities and classrooms were well-maintained and inviting. I look forward to starting my child at this center, knowing they will receive excellent care and a nurturing environment

My daughter has been attending PlayFACTO@Pioneer for over a year, and we couldn’t be happier. She enjoys going there and has never had anything negative to say about the teachers or the environment. The teachers help her with her homework every day, so she finishes it before coming home. This allows us to spend more quality time together in the evenings. The teachers also provide regular updates on her well-being and the progress of her homework via a mobile app. Although the student care fee isn’t the cheapest, the care and attention she receives make it worth every dollar we spent. We’re glad we chose PlayFACTO.

Centre is beautifully renovated, cosy and comfortable.

The timetable and curriculum is holistic to cater to my child learning needs.

The staff is professional and friendly. Ms Junie, the centre head is welcoming and knowledgeable. My girl loves the environment and we're thankful to leave her in good hands. High recommended:)

Junie's hospitality at the student care centre was exceptional! She warmly welcomed us, provided thorough information about the programs, and showed us around with care. Her dedication and knowledge made the decision to enroll our child an easy one. Highly recommended!
Y-shape blocks create interesting courtyard spaces with ponds, landscape, pavilion and play court within. Detailing however could be improved.
Known for their good Thai Food at the canteen at this hall whole fish Asam fish dish only cost $13.90, kerabu mango are their popular dishes.
Wide variety of food choice up till 8pm during school term

Pioneer Hall is more than just a student hostel; it's a nurturing community that supports academic excellence, personal growth, and cultural diversity. Its commitment to providing a comfortable living environment, fostering a strong sense of community, and offering top-notch facilities makes it an ideal choice for students seeking a holistic university experience. I wholeheartedly recommend Pioneer Hall to any student aspiring to thrive academically and socially during their university years at NTU.

Best hall in NTU, 10000/10 would recommend. Amazing atmosphere, amazing people, great hall life. Not to mention a very central location and close proximity to all key spots on campus (SRC, wave, NS, hive, yunnan garden).

Ruoyi has been attending LOL since Primary 5, and we are truly grateful for the guidance and support she has received. Mr Tan has been teaching her Mathematics and Alicia her English — both teachers are very dedicated, patient, and meticulous in their teaching. We have seen good and steady progress in Ruoyi’s results, and more importantly, her confidence has grown significantly.

The centre has excellent control over the entire PSLE revision process, providing clear structure and direction, which puts both students and parents at ease. They not only build up the students’ confidence but also reassure parents throughout this important journey. We are very thankful for the commitment and care shown by the teachers and would highly recommend this tuition centre.

I joined league of learners after I did really badly for my first math weighted assessment with a B4 around March. Mr Quek was really patient and understanding when teaching and kindly repeated the explanations to make sure i understood everything. Although I started 3 months later, I still managed to catch up and was a few chapters ahead of my school teacher. I then had more confidence in myself and was no longer afraid of difficult questions. With all the new knowledge and confidence I brought to my next exam, I used all the methods taught by Mr Quek in the test and got an A1! I felt very happy and thankful for Mr Quek and I would definitely recommend anyone to join league of learners and you will see a big improvement in your results!

May I know what is the relationship between League of Learners and Novelty Learning Center 🤔?

Mr Chiang term it as an "upgrade" of Novelty Learning Center so is Mr Chiang the overall in charge of both League of Learners and Novelty Learning Center?

League of learners tutors are very devoted to the 3 years my 2 child under study english , math A/E and science secondary level with them .
Both of them have achieve results for subjects higher than expected for O and N levels 2019 and 2020 . Credit to the devoted tutors of league of learners .

understanding tutors, engaging lessons, guaranteed results
my sec3 l1r5 was 20+ and i have never been studious type, always asleep in class. But after one year here my sec4 os got 11
highly recommended

I frequent this bus stop as 179 is the only bus from Pioneer that enters NTU. The sign informs commuters of the bus services available at the bus stop

1. Code choice
English is the only language iterated on the sign. This could possibly be due to the limited space on the sign itself, leading to the need to pick a language a majority of commuters would understand.

2. Inscription
The bus sign incorporates mainly shades of green, black font, and yellow. The black font written against the green background highlights the numbers a lot more and the nature of the green background itself captures the attention of commuters.

3. Emplacement
The sign is located at the front of the bus stop and is double-sided so commuters from either side of the bus stop are able to read the information. Its location allows for the commuters to immediately know which bus services are available

The campus rider for NTU starts here. Crowded during peak hours. Can take upto 2 hrs.
The NTU campus rider bus stops for a long time here during peak hours and obstructs public buses.

I waited for the NTU shuttle bus at 8:30 AM on August 6th, and the queue was incredibly long, stretching all the way to the nearby HDB estate. This was during peak hour, yet there was no proper bus frequency control. Buses arrived in clusters instead of regular intervals, leading to massive congestion and delays.

This caused serious disruption to both students and the public. This is not an isolated issue. If NTU wants to maintain its image, this requires urgent attention. For prospective students or visitors—avoid coming between 8:30 AM and 10:00 AM unless you're mentally prepared to queue for 30+ minutes.


This situation not only affects students but also disrupts public order in the surrounding area. It's clearly not an isolated incident but rather a result of poor management. For those who want to apply to NTU, it is recommended to avoid the time period from 8:30 am to 10 am, otherwise you may have a real breakdown if you are not mentally prepared.

Add a boiled egg and the crackle of fresh pappadum, and you’ve got the perfect meal. From across NTU, I walk the 20 minutes—it’s my way of earning the food and making it a healthy ritual. A warm, comforting break from the stress of traffic and daily grind. Hearty, affordable, and packed with flavor—just the recharge I need.

I  brought my child here and saw mold mainly in the ball pit area, so we avoided it and did not visit for a long time. After a recent renovation and the addition of new web play structures, I expected a total renovation and proper cleaning. Apparently not.

However, the situation appears worse. Mold is now visibly present not only in high-contact areas children touch but also on elevated, low-contact surfaces, together with heavy dust accumulation. This indicates a systemic hygiene and moisture-control issue rather than normal wear or “aging fabric.” Several damaged areas are only patched with tape, few digital interactive screens removed / are no longer working, and a new consent form liability waiver has been introduced since many kids injured themselves.

ChatGPT confirmed the presence of mold, accumulation of skin oil, bacteria, dust and dirt over the years without insufficient cleaning regime that creates this situation. It is presenting legitimate health and safety concern. I have attached the screenshot on chatGPT analysis.

The current presence of 2 cleaning lady is too late and insufficient. They need to do total refurbishing and special mold disinfection is required.

My son loves to visit this playground and would come once during sch holidays. The last visit was in Dec2025 and the playground was still lovely, clean and good condition.
Today on 16Jun26, I'm shocked to see  the playground was so messy with toys thrown all over the floor after I entered. The cushioned floor mat looks crumbled. I went to read the recent reviews and the reviews are true. The playground is dirty, mouldy and has a bad smell.
I think this will be my last visit 🥲

A vibrant and well-designed indoor playground that’s perfect for toddlers and younger kids. Yooland at Westgate feels brand new — clean, brightly lit, and packed with interactive zones, including a massive ball pit, mini trampolines, a pretend play area (“Space Kitchen”), and lots of padded play equipment. The outer-space theme adds a playful futuristic vibe.

However, at $25 for just an hour, it’s on the pricey side. That said, you’re paying for a safe, air-conditioned environment with clear zoning, attentive staff, and thoughtful touches like ample seating for parents.

Worth visiting at least once if you’re in the area or looking for a treat day out — just go early to avoid peak crowd times.

We had an amazing time at Yooland! I brought my kids, and they absolutely loved the wide variety of play areas. There’s plenty of space for them to explore, which kept them entertained for hours. The staff was also incredibly friendly and accommodating, making sure we had everything we needed and keeping the environment fun and safe. Overall, it’s a fantastic place to bring kids for a day of active fun. We loved it and will definitely be back.

Visited Yooland on 1st June. It is my first time and my kids really had fun because there were alot of different things to play and the slides were not too steep and scary for them. I also like the toy car rides they have, which allowed them to ride around the indoor playground. However, I cant deny that there were signs of mold on the fabrics around the ballpit. The balls in the ball pit looked worn out and slightly dirty, which made me think if they ever wash or clean it.
The areas at the back where they kept their boxes for storage were in a mess and quite unsightly when my kids ride under the "shelters".
There were also some items damaged and left unattached to its original position, such as an inflated circle item meant to be hung up.
Overall, they need to maintain their cleaning hygiene, especially when it is meant for toddlers and older children.

The playground is clean, tidy and feels very safe for children. The staff are attentive and friendly and they make sure everything is well-maintained. Overall it’s a pleasant and enjoyable place to visit.

Went here a few days ago, the place is small but plenty of hands on activity for my son to try on, the staff is very friendly to all the children and parents. I would love to come by again here for my son birthday soon 😊 thanks to the staff mirra & min for a wonderful day at the playground even tho it's crowded they try their best to serve all customer with a smile 😊

The overall experience was good . I visited the outlet yesterday around 11am and noticed that only one staff member was handling everything. She was managing the cashier and at the same time attending to a crying child even running over to help. I really appreciate her effort as she tried her best to handle multiple responsibilities at once. For parents do not leave your children unattended as it can be difficult for staff to manage everything alone.

Our visit to Capybara kallang was wonderful. The kids had so much fun exploring the different play areas and the variety of toys kept them excited throughout. The space is clean and well-maintained and the layout makes it very easy to keep an eye on the children while they play.

Went on a Thurs morning so there was only 4 other kids & the experience was pleasant

1 hour was sufficient for my tod esp since the place was not as big as expected

Despite the good reviews & 4.6 stars, there were things that can be better/fixed

- there was water at the green stretch between trampolines
- some hair at the sand play area

Overall, my kid had a good time & didn't want to leave.

A pleasant neighbourhood park that’s generally well maintained and easy to enjoy. The greenery is neatly kept, paths are clean, and there’s a good mix of features like open lawns, walking paths, small water features, and basic fitness and play areas. It’s a comfortable space for a casual walk, light exercise, or a short break outdoors

The park is relatively small compared to some of Singapore’s larger parks, so it doesn’t take very long to explore. That said, its compact size also makes it convenient and accessible, especially if you live or work nearby and just want a quick escape into nature without travelling far

Jurong Central Park · Singapore ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Visited in the morning and found it fairly quiet, which made for a pleasant short stroll. The park isn’t particularly spectacular, but it’s a nice spot for light walks and a quick escape into greenery. With the extension currently closed off, the full loop is relatively short at just under 1 km.

What makes the park distinctive are its playful, game-inspired features like the life-sized Snakes and Ladders playground and Ludo garden, alongside ponds and shaded paths. While not a must-visit, it’s a creative and family-friendly community park that adds variety to the West’s green spaces.

Weekend Wanders: Jurong Central Park
Took a stroll through Jurong Central Park over the weekend — a peaceful green escape nestled just minutes away from Jurong Point and Boon Lay MRT.

Though it’s not as expansive as Jurong Lake Gardens, the park offers a refreshing and lush environment that’s perfect for unwinding after a hearty lunch or shopping spree at the mall. With its open lawns, shaded walking paths, and the gentle rustling of trees, it’s a wonderful spot to just pause and breathe.

If you’re around Jurong and need a short nature break, this park is definitely worth a visit. Sometimes, it’s the smaller green spaces that offer the biggest reset.

The park is steeped in history and local knowledge about plants if you know who to look for. Overall also easily 3 degree Celsius cooler.

I spotted a local water hen having a blast and thought it's cute

Jurong Central Park is a well-maintained, family-friendly park in Singapore, featuring creative play areas for children and ample space for walking, running, and jogging.

Some visitors have noted its location in the western part of the island, which may be less convenient for travelers staying in other areas.

Overall, the park is appreciated for its clean environment and suitability for various outdoor activities.
Good office set up , husk external environment .

Very nice place for active treking if you want to escape from the concrete jungle of Singapore. All trails are easy and in very good condition. If you hike or trek in the mountains, you will do this trail in 25% of the estimated time from the info boards. There's a lot of animals there, monkeys are calm, not like these stupid ones from Krabi in Thailand. I really enjoyed a half day there. Take drinks and food with you, since there's no shops near by to purchase anything. You can refill the water at the kayaking station in the south east area of the trail.

Beautiful nature park that links to many trails and walks. Awesome flora and fauna, with a shady canopy most of the way. Convenient parking just at the entrance. Not a big crowd usually. Not as popular as other nature parks such as McRitchie, but not bad at all. Nice cafes and other food options nearby at Upper Thomson too. Would definitely recommend

Found the perfect weekend mini adventure/walk-about in Singapore - a comfortable 5km round trip but extendable for those who wants to go further. Totally immerse in nature throughout the walk, with well spaced intersections of options for a quick turnaround. Highly recommended for low to mid level workout on a sunny weekend.

Just visited Windsor Nature Park and was blown away by how peaceful and lush it is! The trails are well-maintained, with clear signage to guide you through the forest. Loved walking under the canopy of tall trees – it’s so refreshing and a perfect escape from the city buzz. There are also informative boards along the way to learn about local flora and fauna. Great spot for families, hikers, or anyone looking to reconnect with nature. Highly recommend adding this to your list of green spaces to explore in SG!

Good place to relax yourself and regain some positive energy. The walkway is mostly flat, consider safer, but still need to be careful, as there are alot of people exercise/ jogging. You can see greeny plants and cute insects, as well as animals around. And the stream is very clear and clean, can see many fish. Love the air inside, so fresh. Suitable to go alone or in group. And the most important, less mosquitoes inside.

Nice nursery with lots of variety. There is no price tag but the price quoted is consistent and reasonable. They have small plants in front and lot of bigger plants at the back of this nursery. The small plants areas are sheltered but the bigger plants areas mainly exposed to the sun.

They do have nice collection or orchids.

They don't have a huge collection of 'accessories' such pots, fertilizers, treatments etc...

There variety selection of plants to choose.
Affordable price compare to other nurseries around the area too.
Great place to shop,aunty really helpful & friendly.
She help you if you not sure which plant to choose.
There do sell soil & plantation needs too.
To drop by here,if any of you got the chance.
Who knows you might be shopping plants home.

Went over to buy CNY plant. Place is neat and easy to walk around. Price is reasonable, and they have huge variety of plants for you to choose. Only downside is there is no public transport around so if you don't drive that could be a problem.

Found this place by chance and decided to give it a go. Plants and trees are very reasonably priced. Friendly staff. They will drive you around in a buggy if you want to look at more plants as it can be quite hot to walk about. If you have pictures of your pots, they will also suggest what plants to get.

Please be aware of this place. When trolley was pushed to the car, our bag of soil was missing. Promised to give a bag for a free soil, on the way to our car, it went missing as we were walking infront. When we asked it back, they gave us ⅕ of the soil. Not a pleasant customer service

🚤⛵🛳️This Promenade Lookout Jetty with a sweeping view of the stunning shoreline, often moored with ocean-going ships, small colourful fishing boats, stunning yachts and even Jackup Oil Rigs is a popular spot for fishing enthusiast, as well as a great spot to view the sunset.
🏝️West Coast Park which was built on reclaimed land was experiencing coastal erosion, in the early days.
📥To alleviate the erosion at the shoreline, a granite barrier, along with a picturesque promenade was built.
🎣🛟A Lookout Jetty was also included along with the scenic Promenade.

The Promenade Lookout Jetty at West Coast Park is a hidden gem offering a panoramic coastal view that stretches from the Republic of Singapore Yacht Club to Pasir Panjang Terminal and Jurong Island. It's a perfect spot for watching sunsets, with the serene atmosphere enhancing the experience. Photographers will appreciate the expansive views and peaceful surroundings, making it an ideal location for capturing stunning waterfront scenes. The jetty is rarely crowded, providing a tranquil escape where you can relax and take in the beauty of the coast.

Good:

1. A small quiet pier at West Coast Park.
2. Good view of Singapore Port.
3. Golden sunsets on non cloudy days..
4. Police cctv camera give a sense of security.
5. Police patrol duty every  night is very reassuring.
6. Great opportunity for photo ops and drone videography.

Bad:

1. Not a scenic beach. The view in the front is spoiled by cranes and ships.
2. Jetty is very short. Doesn't take you far into the sea.
3. Not ample shelter around. Need to run in case of sudden showers / thunder storms.
4. Do not go down into the water unless an expert swimmer. It is deceptively deep esp during high tide.
5. Lower rocks are exposed during low tide. Don't go down as these can be as slippery as ice.
